title: "This entry does not exist." upon publishing
text: Hi everyone here s an issue I m seeing when publishing entries on the test database publishing seems to erase information Here s an upload I just uploaded and published on the test database https nomad lab eu prod v1 test gui user uploads upload id J YQk4OvQPa5tVLn1rwvEg You can see that there are 3 entries that processed succesfully but when you click on them there s nothing This entry does not exist Before publishing I can click and everything looks normal i e I can see details of the workflow the metadata etc Last time I tried to publish in Feb it worked fine I ve tried multiple times today and I can reproduce the error whether I publish using the API or using the GUI Would anyone know what s happening Hi Thanks for reporting this There must be something bad happening during the publishing phase some file may be dropped out or ends up not being accessible due to errors Let me create an issue about this in Gitlab I can come back to you here once we figure out what is going on Many thanks I ve found out that the entries are still accessible through the API and that when I download the files everything seems to be there So maybe it has more to do with the GUI than the structural integrity of the upload itself Maybe this is related with the DMFT upload Markus Scheidgen There is now an issue about this in GitLab https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2091 Hopefully we find a fix soon Thanks Lauri Himanen Not able to reproduce This particular upload should now work could you Tristan Bereau confirm that everything is as it should The problem seems to be with our system not being able to delete some of the temporary files due to them still being open in some process We will try to investigate this further so that we could prevent this from happening in the future If you encounter this for other of your files you can contact us and we can still always manually do the cleanup Hi Lauri Himanen thanks for the update Unfortunately I m still getting the same problem I ve just uploaded and published this https nomad lab eu prod v1 test gui user uploads upload id n5E4GskfTyadQqeufkpaYQ This one should now work as

title: "This entry does not exist." upon publishing
text: and we can still always manually do the cleanup Hi Lauri Himanen thanks for the update Unfortunately I m still getting the same problem I ve just uploaded and published this https nomad lab eu prod v1 test gui user uploads upload id n5E4GskfTyadQqeufkpaYQ This one should now work as well but unfortunately I can t keep on manually fixing this for you Is there anything special about these entries Do they take a lot of time to process do they contain some specially large files do you have any NORTH tools open when you publish Does it help if you wait for e g 5 min after the parsing before you hit publish This would already give us a hint on solving the problem Oh you fixed that one that s great But yeah this is just to test my workflow so we definitely don t want to have to do this manually The one you just fixed is one of the smallest ones it s around 25kB in size it takes a couple seconds to process and there s no NORTH tools I also have bigger uploads that take up to 15 min to process but I anyway have to wait for the processing to be done before publishing Waiting does not help there s a simulation that had been uploaded yesterday and publishing it right now still leads to the error Thanks This already helps us to track the problem Send me a DM if you still want me to apply the manual fix to some uploads but I can t promise to do it regularly Thanks a lot for your help I don t think I ll need manual fixes for now I can still query the metadata and download the files through the NOMAD API

title: .hdf5 files (own made .h5, .h5iona, ...) for publication on central nomad
text: I have to upload all sorts of hdf5 files own made h5 h5iona to central nomad for publication I am currently developing schemas to process the uploaded files to be able to extract results but also to get access to the Publish section in the Overview of each upload So I am spending hours to delete old uploads uploading the new hdf5 archive yaml files and clicking some magic workarounds https discord com channels REDACTED PHONE REDACTED PHONE REDACTED PHONE to finally see if the import does work Is there a more automated and efficient way to do the above described procedure while testing schemas with the web ui of central NOMAD Hi Wilfried I can think of a way to automate the process You can use the api directly to upload your files easily All you need is a token and you are all set http localhost 8000 fairdi nomad latest api v1 extensions docs auth get token auth token post the you can use cURL or any other packages to create a new upload using this endpoint curl X POST http nomad lab eu prod v1 api v1 uploads token token T local file I have attached the files for you to test you need to zip them before sending There I have the logic of data and schema separated In this way we are creating an entry given that schema in the yaml file You can also use json file if it is more convenient Let me know if you any issues Hope this helps with automating the process Amir Golparvar is it possible to store the chemical formula in your example to make this entry searchable with the Elements Formula filter int the main GUI Is there any doc about that in order to make the elements searchable through the periodic table from Elements Formula you would need to use the results section normalizer which Alvin Noe Ladines could help but the way you are using the data section it is not possible to achieve that via the central nomad for now unless we define a new app to make the custom quantity searchable Maybe Lauri Himanen can comment better on the resulta normalizer I found this example which helped me a bit further https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad docs blob add60881b95815b1a22dc88db5777df9aa52235c examples data custom schema simple schema archive yaml Wilfried Hortschitz In general it

schemas You may be able to do it e g by using the nomad datamodel metainfo basesections v2 ElementalComposition section in your schema E g like this yaml definitions name hdf5 sections Sample base sections nomad datamodel data EntryData nomad datamodel metainfo basesections HDF5Normalizer quantities datafile default test h5 type str m annotations eln component FileEditQuantity charge density type np float32 shape m annotations hdf5 path Fe spx BS output charge density total sub sections elemental composition repeats true section base sections nomad datamodel metainfo basesections v2 ElementalComposition And then populating the data with something like yaml data m def upload raw h5 schema archive yaml Sample datafile test h5 elemental composition element C element H Dear Lauri Himanen thank you for your help I could in the meantime adopt the above mentioned example which I want o post here since it is a bit different to your solution and maybe helpful for others Like all schemas the information goes into the definitions section of the archive definitions Schema have a name name NOMAD Example Lab Schema packages contain section definitions This is wear the interesting schema information begins sections Here we define a section called Chemical simple schema Section definition can have base sections Base sections are other schema definition and all properties of these will be inherited base sections Provides typical quantities like name descriptions chemical formula and makes those available for search nomad datamodel metainfo eln Chemical Declares this as a top level entry section This determines the types of entries you can create With this we will be able to create a Chemical entry nomad datamodel data EntryData data The data section contains the actual data It is a dictionary with key value pairs The keys are the names of the quantities and the values are the values of

title: .hdf5 files (own made .h5, .h5iona, ...) for publication on central nomad
text: entries you can create With this we will be able to create a Chemical entry nomad datamodel data EntryData data The data section contains the actual data It is a dictionary with key value pairs The keys are the names of the quantities and the values are the values of the quantities m def simple schema The chemical formula quantity is set to H2O chemical formula H2O You are getting into a territory where we are pushing the limits of what you can do with the YAML schemas I would also prefer parsers but we have been told that yaml schemas are the only way to currently share custom hdf5 data set on central nomad Thanks again for your help Wouldn t it be possible to fill the results section directly in the YAML pertaining to the data Something like this data m def upload raw h5 schema archive yaml Sample datafile test h5 results material elemental composition element C element H There is an h5oina parser in pynxtools em Thanks for the info but to use that on central nomad to get publishable datasets I tried uploading an h5oina central nomad stable and the dataset was not recognized The results section is designed to be filled automatically by a parser or a normalize function This gives us the option to later modify and restructure the results section the parser normalize function and do a reprocessing that follows that new structure If we would allow encourage people to fill the results section manually we would not be able to re process these entries as easily in the future Changed the channel name hdf5 files own made h5 h5iona for publication on central nomad

title: 502 Bad Gateway on north app
text: buffers 8 16k proxy busy buffers size 64k ahmed ilyas thanks that did it Pepe Marquez The private keycloak was the outcome of an discussion I had with our IT However this Helmholtz ID and the fact that we are already connected to this is new to me and my IT collegue MIght reconsider Pepe Marquez FYI I also installed a custom keycloak recently for an oasis in order flexibly restrict the user base Using the whitelist in nomad yaml always requires restarting the oasis upon any change unfortunately so this was not really an option for a flexible user base I also wanted to use that same user registry for other services as well Sascha Klawohn Lauri Himanen some food for thought for our keyclock and user management support It also comes very clearly to me that users still do not know that they can login with their institutional accounts Thank you Laurenz Rettig I suspect that this might add barriers when transferring data across installations but is very valuable for us to know I wanted to connect my local keycloak to the central nomad instance to avoid such problems but did not get any feedback on whether this is possible https discord com channels REDACTED PHONE REDACTED PHONE Just to add another limitation If you are behind a proxy for Jupyter in the NORTH hub it seems not possible to pass the proxy settings to Jupyter at least Micha gave up on that As Juypter needs the keycloak to work this might also a reason why you want to have an internal keycloak if bypassing the proxy is not allowed by your IT People are probably confused by the name of the service Helmholtz ID AAI including ORCID That s the label on the login button The button name we can change at any point Should we just make it into something like Institutional login including ORCID Any title: Apps dissapear in NOMAD Oasis 1.3.15
text: I updated my Oasis from 1 3 13 to 1 3 15 latest and I discovered that all apps that are defined in plugins dissapeared Only the apps I have defined in my nomad yaml old style show up After reverting back to 1 3 13 the apps are available again Any fix Lauri Himanen Do you have the entry points deactivated in the nomad yaml I have not done anything regarding the entry points So far updating to other NOMAD versions did not make them dissapear There was a fix at some point because excluded apps were not actually excluded So could be that you always had them excluded and only now did it take effect Can you DM me the nomad yaml with the secrets removed yes this is correct and this is a valuable fix in 1 3 15 you need to explicitly enable APPs otherwise all available apps are shown which was a mess I see I actually liked how it was before like listen the apps up the same way I do for schemas under plugins include or in ui apps include the issue before was that in production all apps in a plugin were displayed also those who are not relevant for the oasis which was really annoying because some apps did not work because some schemas were not active in an oasis now we have much more control and yes you can enable apps the same way as schemas and parsers plugins We only include our schema here Without the explicit include all plugins will be loaded Many build in plugins require more dependencies Install nomad lab parsing to make all default plugins work entry points include nomad tfsc general parsers tfsc general experiment experiment parser nomad tfsc general schema packages tfsc general package nomad tfsc general apps tfsc voila documentation app thanks i also think that everything is enabled as long as you dont use an include section in the entrypoints that is what I also thinking I think I might get rid of the include section nowadays since we can now better control the Oasis image by including only the plugins we want yes that is possible my plugins ahve 10 or more entrypoints and i dont want all of them always activated

title: Archive-Files and Units
text: I am coming back to an issue I discussed with several people at the FAirmat Meeting Right now if I download an archive file of my data from Nomad I have no units Also if the data get published and some else would like to download my data they get data without units my understanding I believe this needs to be changed urgently such that if I download an archive file with e g a two column data wavelength and signal they have units There seems to be a different API archive call possible to achieve this but this runs apparently only on unix machines Agreed having no units in archive files was one not the exclusive but one of the reasons why we in Area B e g had to add the cumbersome effectively almost doubling of archive information via an eln data yaml Without units in an archive file the entire unit contextualization is assumed left to be within the RDMS but more subtle that RDMS in a specific version at least if not even environment with is version because of which at all knowledge about which units exists and how to interconvert between different ones is technically possible Every serialized artifact exportable via GUI functionalities from NOMAD should have units for each quantity and if not users should assume the unit is UNITLESS There is no point in FAIRmat trying to convince technology partners to become more explicit about the units they use when we aren t either ThomasU that should be made an issue on nomad FAIR hey the units are stored in the metainfo and can be accessed through the gui or with a more or less easy python script see attachment i agree that nomad could provide an easier way for this alternatively one can also load the archive in nomad directly not via requests but with the nomad archive queries right then the archive would contain the actual pint quantities since it is possible already at the moment i wouldnt think that this is an urgent issue I can show you how to integrate it in your scripts if you like There are two separate issues As Micha is correctly writing the units are stored in the schema i e the metainfo I can understand the reason why someone want to have the unit there but we cannot blindly repeat the unit in

title: Archive-Files and Units
text: integrate it in your scripts if you like There are two separate issues As Micha is correctly writing the units are stored in the schema i e the metainfo I can understand the reason why someone want to have the unit there but we cannot blindly repeat the unit in each archive because NOMAD contains 24 5 billion individual quantities and repeating unit 24 5 times is quite expensive For similar reasons you won t find default values in the archive Its not quite as obvious as Markus Kuehbach makes it seem What might be possible is that we transparently add the unit information when the archive is requested via API This might be an issue we could do something about Sometimes you might need to store a unit in the archive because you either do not know it when you write the schema or want to deviate from the unit in the schema Here you would need to add the unit as a piece of data Specifically for nexus and area B we added a feature to the metainfo that allowed to specify that a quantity should have a flexible unit I am not sure if and how this is used Mabye it needs to be generalized Again this might be an issue where we could actually do something about I cleaned a bit the file This has a function to extract the unit from artifact js Not storing units inside the RDMS as an optimization strategy is all good as the RDMS takes care of that contextualization Export of contextualized pieces of information out of an RDMS be it for Oasis or for whichever RDMS is for which having serialization and contextualization export functionality available can be useful This was my point I probably do not understand the technical problem yet From my lay perspective an archive file contains a huge amount of information which to me seems superfluous As an example I have a file with conductivity values for position x y these are stored in the json in the data key data m def hzb combinatorial libraries schema packages hzb library package UnoldConductivityMeasurementLibrary name HZB FaAk 20240619 4030 12 datetime REDACTED PHONE 19T15 10 00 00 00 method Conductivity Measurement Mapping data file HZB FaAk 20240619 4030 12 Resistance2024 06 19 1710 csv samples name HZB FaAk 20240619 4030 12 reference uploads JDw05AQWRlq4O1HJaWEFRg archive mtAzfMKH2IAFxkyJNPw3iVhvXyUT data

title: Base section inheritance for synthesis precursors of a composite system
text: Hi I am trying to figure out how to define a schema for nanoparticle synthesis and characterization My understanding of the CompositeSystem base section is that the components define the elemental composition of the final material not the initial starting materials Ideally I would like to define the precursors as PureSubstances since they would likely have CAS or PubChem references Would it make sense to define a collection of precursor PureSubstances and add an additional reference to a Process object that describes the synthesis How have other experiment schemas described processes like synthesis Thanks Hi Erin Mee Hampus Näsström might be able to help you here Hi Erin Sounds like you are on the right way We are in the process of refactoring these basesections to better integrate with the computation data in NOMAD However the new sections don t yet have any of the normalization functionality implemented You can therefore choose if you want to start using the old sections and the migrate or already start using the new ones But in principle it is very similar and what you explained above sounds very reasonable This week I m a bit stressed for time but i would love to hear more about your use case Thank you I appreciate the help Also if it makes any difference this is experimental data where we are synthesizing the nanoparticles then characterizing them with UV Vis and SAXS but I assume in principle it is the same

title: Cannot access uploads, because of error: Could not validate credentials. The given token is invalid.
text: zone issue E g our user management and your oasis not running in sync Your laptop being behind When you open a new tab you start a new instance of the gui and it will get a new potentially out of time token So every time you open a new tab there is the potential for this to happen Could you check your os time settings I am not sure what the Gateway timeout problem is Is the gateway timeout coming from keycloak or the nomad api Perfect I think the time issue was indeed the problem I went to the OS settings and synchronized the clock again From then on I did not yet get the validation error message again This also explains why it sometimes worked and sometimes not because from time to time the OS is synchronizing the clock automatically Thank you very much The Gateway Timeout Error is coming from the NOMAD API But in the container logs there is no information about it Maybe it was due to the Validation error e g if I had no valid token I was not allowed to access some entries and run into a timeout I will keep an eye on that

original parser anymore and nothing gets parsed Still no answer in here but I also found out that for archive json files if there are underscores for example in the name or lab id they just get removed whenever I save Hi weidtn Sorry I must have overlooked I am guessing you are overwriting the mainfile in your parser Right now NOMAD does not work with having EditQuantity fields in your schema and modifying the data On triggering save action it will overwrite the mainfile You probably have to switch to use normalize method to enable using editquantities in your schema The underscore object and not the value Thats indeed does not look ok I will have a look One other way indeed to use the EditQuantity is to have archive json yaml for sure We have some plans to allow editquantities to be used in parsing stage but would be in the future At least it s in here now for future reference I think it should always create a new archive whenever I parse something that should be editable title: Complex Numbers cannot be displayed in Front-end
text: We traced back part of the section card loading error to a failed matrix element for complex numbers It seems that when a complex number quantity is produced it is being displayed as a matrix heatmap This is happens under PropertyPreview line 120 in components entry properties SectionCard js It shows that the format conversion is not proper yielding rather two dictionaries of arrays than the expected array This then raises a TypeError when applying length in Matrix rowCount line 119 in components archive visualizations js This bug was traced by Vikrant Chaudhary and myself He will shortly post the error trace and conflicting schema definition Note that the quantity is directly under archive data As for patching it Firstly is it wishful to showcase complex numbers as matrices heatmaps Secondly could we fix the conversion of the of the complex numbers between back end and front end Debug print out quantityDef and section console log Matrix Call quantityDef section Debug print out values shape pages and current sliced values console log Matrix Debug values shape pages pageLengths Conflicting schema Quantity definition shc components Quantity type np complex128 shape n points n components description Complex SHC values for different components like xyz xxy etc a display visible False This error is shown on GUI I am using distro dev If works fine if complex numbers are not parsed

1834a0353728a4d8418522cef4f5b24af956e756 src nomad catalysis parsers catalysis parsers py L42 that approach does not use the child archives arguments at all Thank you Julia Schumann for the solution normalizers as well Hampus Näsström might be better suited to respond to your questions yrrliu I m sorry for the delayed response I was on vacation We are working on formalizing this method and providing more documentation for it For now we have introduced the update entry context which

text: for normalizers as well Hampus Näsström might be better suited to respond to your questions yrrliu I m sorry for the delayed response I was on vacation We are working on formalizing this method and providing more documentation for it For now we have introduced the update entry context which provides the functionality of the create archive function Julia linked to you I m hoping to get something ready for the documentation soonish You can find the very preliminary documentation on the update entry context here https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad docs blob main docs howto develop normalizing md Hi Hampus Näsström thanks for the solution So if I understand correctly this should be the parsing workflow 1 Use MyMatchingParser to parse the main YAML file my parent mainfile yaml 2 This main YAML file contains instructions for parsing auxiliary files 3 Parse the auxiliary files according to these instructions and save the parsing results as JSON YAML files e g my children 1 mainfile yaml using archive m context update entry 4 When calling update entry with the argument process True it will automatically trigger another parsing step 5 In this additional parsing step MyChildrenMatchingParser will be used to parse the newly created YAML files my children 1 mainfile yaml as regular main files If the workflow above is true I have some follow up questions 1 How I need title: Creating multiple uploads causes some to get stuck "Waiting"

s strange that it always works when using the GUI Might be that the CURL method for some reason takes a bit more memory and crashes because of that But maybe first confirm if it really is caused by memory problems So this we would do by just looking at top while uploading one That is what I would do there are probably a lot better ways ChatGPT would know Hahaha Yeah we were down at 100 mb So probably that is it It s a bit strange that the system is using 6GB in idle If there is a problem with the Oasis memory being filled up over time even when no processing is happening there could be a memory leak somewhere Should this not be reset with a docke compose down and up Sounds about right I m also at 6Gb when running the infrastructure browser with a few tabs I m at 10 haha Yes the memory will be reset if you reboot the containers But NOMAD is not using too much of that But okay Maybe 8Gb is just not enough Most of it is ES Mongo RabbitMQ python etc Maybe we need to revise the We recommend at least 2GB per core and a minimum of 8GB in the docs But thank you Lauri That seems to indeed be the problem so we will ask for more RAM In production we use something like 128Gb for heavy processing But this all depends on what sort of data you are dealing with and how smart the parsers are Is it a bug that these uploads get stuck in Waiting forever Yes I think you could call it a bug If the worker crashes there is probably no easy way to somehow know that the status should be reset Maybe we could run some cleanup job at regular intervals That would be very good Because right now it gets stuck and you can t even delete the upload without running the nomad admin reset processing Unfortunately the system that runs the upload processing is not super safe And we occasionally have issues here The upload processing is one celery

title: Creating multiple uploads causes some to get stuck "Waiting"
text: That would be very good Because right now it gets stuck and you can t even delete the upload without running the nomad admin reset processing Unfortunately the system that runs the upload processing is not super safe And we occasionally have issues here The upload processing is one celery task It matches files and then spawns new celery tasks for each entry The problem is the mechamsim that waits for all entries to complete There is no good primitive for this in celery What we ended up doing is that each entry task is checking if it was the last entry that finished If an entry thinks it was the last it marks the upload as completed This is also one reason why we generally do not allow to trigger a new processing while still running an old processing If one of the entry processing does fail so severely that i cannot mark itself as completed then we never have a last entry finishing and the processing usually gets stuck This for example might happen if the celery work dies unexpectedly In principle celery has a mechanism to even do something with tasks when the work dies but this does not seem to work relyably In my experience when the kernel kills a celery worker e g to much ram use it usually gets stuck in this state There is no good solution at the moment After the GUI the processing is the next thing that needs some refactoring

fine It could be that there is some sort of memory bug or something that is subtle but in my experience the

title: DEV Deployment stalls on upload
text: think there is anything extraordinary about these uploads The size may be larger than a typical DFT calculation but in most cases uploads of this size are processing fine It could be that there is some sort of memory bug or something that is subtle but in my experience the hanging is somewhat rare and random so I wouldn t really want to spend time digging into this Perhaps we can start keeping a more detailed log of these issues to try to find any correlations These are now removed

the link it results in DOI Not Found on the doi webpage Any ideas what the problem is here If you have just published maybe it just takes a little while On the website it states a reason could be The DOI has not been activated yet Please try again later and report the problem if the error continues I thought that at first but the FAQ I am looking at says it takes on the order of an hour but it was activated this morning already OK I am not sure how long it took when I did this for my dataset But it is working now so it should not be a general thing from the website https doi org REDACTED PHONE NOMAD REDACTED PHONE 2 ok good to know thanks I will try to be patient Hi Julia Schumann thanks for your input It seems like the link is still broken today https dx doi org REDACTED PHONE NOMAD REDACTED PHONE 1 ok this is really interesting yesterday i published also a dataset on an oasis with doi and it seems to have gotten the same doi as you REDACTED PHONE NOMAD REDACTED PHONE 1 Markus Scheidgen Lauri Himanen The DOI s are issued by a third party solution doi org Sometimes there are issues in this service and the DOI request never goes through properly So I think in this case the DOI service failed to produce the DOI correctly but NOMAD still thinks that it is fine That is probably also the reason for the duplication you both got a non working DOI since the system gave an initial ID for you both but which never really got registered fully I think Markus Scheidgen has some typical workaround for this since he has the credentials to the service Collecting here a list of problematic links https dx doi org REDACTED PHONE NOMAD REDACTED PHONE 1 https dx doi org REDACTED PHONE NOMAD REDACTED PHONE 2 also created yesterday before I posted this I will have a look at those I am sorry that this happens again and again It seems to turn into a regular problem and maybe we need to improve

title: Distribution and measurements are incompatible due to pynxtools versions
text: I m preparing for the workshop and forked FAIRmat NFDI nomad distro dev After running git submodule update init recursive I ve added two plugins git submodule add https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad measurements git packages nomad measurements git submodule add https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad material processing git packages nomad material processing I ve refrained form adding the electronic parsers as it s listed as legacy Running uv add packages nomad measurements packages nomad material processing fails with No solution found when resolving dependencies Because nomad distribution depends on pynxtools apm 0 10 0 and nomad measurements depends on pynxtools 0 10 6 we can conclude that nomad distribution and nomad measurements are incompatible And because your workspace requires nomad distribution and nomad measurements dev we can conclude that your workspace s requirements are unsatisfiable help If you want to add the package regardless of the failed resolution provide the frozen flag to skip locking and syncing nomad distro dev had this MR https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad distro dev pull 47 bumping pynxtools from 0 9 1 to 0 10 0 while it was title: Docker Issues with first start up of NOMAD Oasis
text: Hi all I am trying to get nomad oasis installed on my computer I cloned the nomad distro template repository at https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad distro template tree main and am following the installation steps here https nomad lab eu prod v1 docs howto oasis install html When I run docker compose up I am unable to get the app container to start up I am using a mac computer and have allocated 10GB to container memory use Here are some of the issues in the message log ERROR Worker pid 176 was sent SIGKILL Perhaps out of memory I am also getting the following messages The following unsupported keys were found in your configuration e g nomad yaml enable name mkdir p failed for path nonexistent config matplotlib Hi Erin 10 GB should in theory be enough but it depends on how many cores you are running I think it should still work on startup though Is this on the new apple silicon architecture I just tried it on a Linux distribution Fedora and it started fine Maybe ahmed ilyas can give it a try as I think he runs a Mac I m guessing this is only for testing For actually setting up your own Oasis we highly recommend actually using the GitHub template and creating your own repository I can also recommend creating your own repository and removing the set of plugins that you don t need from the toml table That should decrease the memory requirements Thank you removing the plugins worked

title: Error in m_to_dict()
text: I am getting the error AttributeError str object has no attribute m path see full output attached supposedly during archive writing I have narrowed down the issue to a particular subsection of the archive RadiusOfGyrationValues defined in dependencies schemas simulation run runschema calculation py It appears to be a fundamental issue with this section since it happens for 2 distinct examples The populated sections looks like m def runschema calculation RadiusOfGyrationValues Section m parent RadiusOfGyration kind radius of gyration values m parent sub section runschema calculation RadiusOfGyration radius of gyration values SubSection m parent index 0 m context None m mod count 4 m cache m annotations atomsgroup ref AtomsGroup x h5md parameters label type index composition formula n atoms atom indices is molecule atoms group label 1ZNF index 0 value 8 601769825009057e 10 The atomsgroup ref is not the issue so I presume it has to do with value or label I think this is related to an issue Sarthak Kapoor had last week He was able to resolve it so maybe he can provide some insight this seems to be related to a missing context you see in this piece of the error sh File work soft nomad nomad metainfo metainfo py 549 in Reference serialize impl self section value 545 def serialize impl self section value 546 return append id 547 value m path 548 if context section m root m context is None 549 else context create reference section self definition value 550 value 551 it returns value m path which is a str in case the context found is None This propagates the error if your goal is to create an archive you can try wrapping the section in entry archive while providing the context before using m to dict Something like py entry EntryArchive data your section m context archive m context entry m to dict Thanks for the replies Sorry one aspect may have been unclear The original error is occurring in the GUI during processing of an upload I recreated the error by running the parser alone locally and then calling m to dict on the problematic section This is the original GUI error i see then i am not sure what could be the issue ok thanks anyway Alvin Noe Ladines Any ideas about this issue it is one of the Reference quantities that is somehow not resolved can you determine

Reference quantities that is somehow not resolved can you determine which quantity this is perhaps print the name or something ah ok I did verify that it is the atomsgroup ref quantity that is causing the problem Thanks for pointing that out I still don t understand the issue After parsing my archive is correctly populated and the reference is resolved properly in python python a 0 run 0 calculation 0 radius of gyration 0 radius of gyration values 0 atomsgroup ref yields AtomsGroup x h5md parameters label type index composition formula n atoms atom indices is molecule atoms group and I can even run m to dict on this section so the inner contents are fine But if I run m to dict on a 0 run 0 calculation 0 radius of gyration 0 radius of gyration values 0 I get the AttributeError str object has no attribute m path error Could it be something in the quantity definition Here are the relevant classes python class AtomicGroupValues MSection Generic section containing information regarding the values of a trajectory property m def Section validate False label Quantity type str shape description Describes the atoms or molecule types involved in determining the property atomsgroup ref Quantity type Reference AtomsGroup m def shape 1 description References to the atoms group section containing the molecule for which Rg was calculated class RadiusOfGyrationValues AtomicGroupValues Section containing information regarding the values of radius of gyration Rg m def Section validate False value Quantity type np dtype np float64 shape unit m description Value of Rg i do not see any problem with the def i have a feeling that this is a metainfo problem somehow maybe Theodore Chang DM BITTE can comment it could also be that the archive context is missing Solved thanks to Theodore s help SOLUTION reference quantity is defined as a list but was being stored directly as a single reference

title: Exact str match of schema quantity via API
text: I have a problem by requesting entries via the API I use a query like this python query required metadata data owner visible query and results eln sections any PVcomB HFDip search quantities id data process last HF change nomad pvcomb schema packages pvcomb schema package PVcomB HFDip datetime value REDACTED PHONE 01T10 16 00 000Z search quantities id data process used tank nomad pvcomb schema packages pvcomb schema package PVcomB HFDip str value tank pagination order by upload create time order desc page size 50 The quantity used tank is type str It can be different tanks like wet bench tank and manual tank If I now overhand manual tank to the query it also replies entries where tank is wet bench tank So the str search seems to behave like and and therefore matches tank I guess If I overhand e g beaker it works correctly I already tried several things like str value exact and search quantities all but this gives errors Is there any solution for that we figured out that the trick is python tank manual tank query owner visible query entry type PVcomB HFDip upload id 3HK8UmRCR3OhVqQySD1kAA search quantities id data process used tank nomad pvcomb schema packages pvcomb schema package PVcomB HFDip str value all tank split which is really nice it is good to know that single words are indexed

title: Fail to launch jupyter hub on oasis
text: We are trying to launch a jupyter hub on an oasis and have checked that the docker user is the default 991 that is set in the docker compose file but when we try launch the jupyter hub we get a key error in the nomad oasis app worker Can you find upload PZcawRGJQ3a wpXVYyTj6w in volumes Does volumes have the correct access rights sudo chown R 1000 volumes I think Adam Fekete might have more tips Unfortunately I am no longer on site with the users so will take a bit of time to check this but I ll ask the IT admin For my understanding is it that it tries to mount these folders in the jupyter container and then can t access them But this comes from the nomad app not the jupyterhub The stacktrace indicates that this error occures when the app tries to collect all upload directories before it sends them to jupyterhub In other words the jupyterhub is not jet involved It sounds more like a file permission problem as Lauri says You sould docker exec into the app container and check does the upload directory exist in volumes fs staging and only there not again in volumes fs public does the upload directory belong to user 1000 does the app actually run as user 1000 You can also run the nomad admin uploads integrity missing storage command in the container to check if there are uploads with missing or inaccesible directories

title: Failing test in plugin

the parse command in the github pipeline Does anyone have hints on how to solve this problem Sarthak Kapoor Lauri Himanen Thank you FYI Sathya Sai Seetharaman I think the error points out that the schema definition is not available I couldn t find any problem with the loading of the schema entry point One thing I have not seen used before in schemas is overriding the init method as done here https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad schema plugin nmr blob 824fa87a6c395ecf77867f6b89d7a44fb90e095b src nomad nmr schema schema packages schema package py L370 Maybe this is causing the schema not to be loaded properly Can you try defining the init method as py def init self args kwargs super init args kwargs self rank 3 3 Also as you want to only set a property you could skip using init method and make it part of the normalize method py def normalize self archive EntryArchive logger BoundLogger None super normalize archive logger self rank 3 3 Hi Sarthak thanks i think i tried to remove it but this was not the problem I solved in the past some errors changing version for several area C packages loaded Maybe Sathya Sai Seetharaman could look around if every package in the dependecies is at its last version Hi Sarthak Kapoor and Andrea Albino I have dug into the error a bit more The difference seems to be because I declared a unit for MagneticSusceptibility in the class In the original repo as you have created it the value is declared with unit being dimensionless https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad schema plugin nmr blob 824fa87a6c395ecf77867f6b89d7a44fb90e095b src nomad nmr schema schema packages schema package py L362 When I changed the unit to be consistent with literature the error started popping up My value definition looks like this now value Quantity type np float64 unit 10 6 cm 3 mol description Value of the macroscopic magnetic susceptibility tensor I tried changing the unit back to dimensionless locally and it seemed to fix the error Any ideas why Hi Sathya Sai Seetharaman very nice that you spotted it I don t know exactly the reason but you could try removing the unit attribute

title: Failing test in plugin
text: of the macroscopic magnetic susceptibility tensor I tried changing the unit back to dimensionless locally and it seemed to fix the error Any ideas why Hi Sathya Sai Seetharaman very nice that you spotted it I don t know exactly the reason but you could try removing the unit attribute completely and check if the error persists My guess is that the way you define the units is not being parsed correctly by pint Can you test parsing it into pint on python REPL Alternatively you can compose units using SI base units This is the convention we usually follow So for your case it will be m 3 mol The display units can be modified to cm 3 mol if you want to add custom units i think you will have to first add it to the pint registry When testing on REPL make sure to use import units registry from nomad units I can also test it once i am on my desk in sometime I tested with this py from nomad units import ureg print ureg Unit 10 6 cm 3 mol and get the following error ValueError Unit expression cannot have a scaling factor which is to be expected Same error also pops up when defining a quantity with such units py from nomad metainfo import Quantity value Quantity type float unit 10 6 cm 3 mol Sathya Sai Seetharaman Can you test if removing the scaling factor in your case fixes the issue Hi Sarthak Kapoor Thank you for the great shout I simply translated the units from the magres specification document as they are found in the magres file which we parse I did make sure that the units I defined were found in the pint repo definitions but it makes sense that the scaling factor is unexpected When I declare the unit as m 3 mol it works fine In the test data and test script I should also change the test value It is just a single float value at the moment but the expected input in reality is a 3 3 tensor I intend to define something like n11 n12 n13 n21 n22 n23 n31 n32 n33 When parsed this is how we would store the value anyway Have you got any feedback to define it better or is assumption alright I ll keep you posted how it goes anyway Follow

title: Failing test in plugin
text: tensor I intend to define something like n11 n12 n13 n21 n22 n23 n31 n32 n33 When parsed this is how we would store the value anyway Have you got any feedback to define it better or is assumption alright I ll keep you posted how it goes anyway Follow up I have changed the test value in the YAML file and the test py script to a tensor as expected from a magres file refer here https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad schema plugin nmr pull 1 commits d632ab1f82af149a069127a528800be4051a304b All tests have passed now Thank you for your help This issue can be closed now I think I just have one more question should there be tests added for every quantity like the magnetic shielding electric field gradient etc I assume so but would like your advice Thanks again Looks excellent Extending the tests is always a good idea especially when adding a feature or changing the data model The test itself acts as the first demo for how the feature datamodel is supposed to be used Great Will add that to the list as well Thank you Hi all I need a little bit of clarity on something Sorry I got a little side track in the last week so couldn t get to this sooner In the https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad schema plugin nmr blob schema package add src nomad nmr schema schema packages schema package py mainly the resolve name from entity ref where the name is resolved for each atomic site from the input list of entities the nomads simulations schema packages atoms state AtomsState is used to read chemical symbols and optionally an index field is initialised but not used currently I understand this is to stay in line with some magres file examples to label each atomic site as hydrogen atoms H 1 H 2 etc and carbon atoms C 1 C 2 etc for example But recently someone in the community has raised the possibility that in some DFT codes you can assign custom site labels based on atomic symmetry If a few H atoms correspond to a certain symmetry there could be four different H atoms labelled H1 or even H1a which can be further resolved to unique sites as H1 1 H1 2 etc So I need a way to record these custom labels when storing the tensors

title: Failing test in plugin
text: If a few H atoms correspond to a certain symmetry there could be four different H atoms labelled H1 or even H1a which can be further resolved to unique sites as H1 1 H1 2 etc So I need a way to record these custom labels when storing the tensors plus indices I noticed that another quantity nomad datamodel metainfo aystem Atoms labels from the metainfo library may be suitable for this Am I allowed to use this or was this reserved for the older data schema Follow up question If I am working with custom data labels such as this H1 H1a etc would it still be possible to extract the unique elements symbols from this list to record This will tie into chemical symbols being used to filter out results and extracting chemical formulas right Esma Boydas maybe you are more familiar with this atomsstate class Hi Sathya Sai Seetharaman indeed the nomad datamodel metainfo aystem Atoms labels belongs to the older schema and we are currently updating the nomad simulations In the new schema we could easily utilize this under AtomsState atom labels or at the top level ModelSystem particle labels In both cases it is trivial to extract the unique elemental symbols from that list May I first ask which version of nomad simulations are you using and how urgent is this Update on the current state of affairs I will branch off from our latest release 0 4 0 and incorporate these custom labels in the schema Stay tuned Hi all this post can be closed as resolved Many thanks for all your guidance The original problem and all subsequent problems raised have now been solved

title: Failure to parse simulations from the newest GROMACS version
text: nomad lab eu https nomad lab eu fails to parse my uploaded GROMACS 2024 2 simulations Link to GROMACS parser https github com nomad coe atomistic parsers blob develop atomisticparsers gromacs parser py due to an error with MDAnalysis which is used to parse tpr files see attached screenshot The problem is that the used MDAnalysis version does not support the most recent tpr tpx file format However since the most recent version of MDAnalysis seems to support it see here https github com MDAnalysis mdanalysis blob 489905f613b39f6a546217867be3f3d7841294a8 package MDAnalysis topology tpr setting py L41 an upgrade of the MDAnalysis library would probably solve the problem Hi Luis Walter and thanks for the report Indeed that sounds like a problem we could solve with a library update Joseph Rudzinski you know the GROMACS parser quite well do you think we could simply try to update MDAnalysis to solve this Thanks for tagging me Lauri Himanen Luis Walter I think the best approach would be for me to first make a quick fix to disable the reading of FF information for the newest gromacs version the extracted FF info is currently not very useful anyway we are planning to improve this in the near future Then I will open an issue to investigate upgrading MDAnalysis as this could cause issues downstream I will post here once I make the change Does it sound ok for you Luis Walter Tristan Bereau So it turns out that we are actually using the latest release of MDAnalysis 2 7 0 to my knowledge Unfortunately this does not yet support Gromacs 2024 This means that I will have to disable some features I don t think this will affect you too much for the paper off the top of my head it means rdfs msds rgs will not be calculated ff interactions will not be stored although as I said before these are incomplete anyway so I wouldn t take this into account Hopefully there are no other side effects we will have to test it Is it out of the question to repeat the calculations with the previous version of Gromacs short follow up indeed this now causes an issue with the system visualization I can reach out to the MDAnalysis devs to see what their plans are for this but I think we should rerun with Gromacs 2023 for the paper this is

title: Failure to parse simulations from the newest GROMACS version
text: calculations with the previous version of Gromacs short follow up indeed this now causes an issue with the system visualization I can reach out to the MDAnalysis devs to see what their plans are for this but I think we should rerun with Gromacs 2023 for the paper this is what Tristan used for his calculations I think the MDAnalysis Code on Github already supports tpx version 133 that s what I linked in the initial issue message but it is not part of the current latest release from December 2023 Rerunning everything us possible but takes some time oh i see in any case we need to wait for a stable version to have robust usage and testing so this will not work for us for the time being talk to Tristan about it and we can meet maybe next week if we need to discuss together Thanks Joseph Rudzinski sounds like we should indeed avoid Gromacs 2024 for now Np sorry to deliver the bad news

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text: if I send a GET request for an upload that is still processing it corrupts it Has anyone been able to reproduce this You need to POST the upload receive the upload id and immediately send a GET request for that upload id Right now I need to have my code wait a few seconds hope that the processing is done and then query it But because I don t know how long the processing will take this can be problematic An example of this can be found here https nomad lab eu prod v1 test gui user uploads upload id q HvqWzAR8WIfkdai1z2Nw entry id sZZ mSvZsUqTumsz5M iJrQWxYSs One of the entries failed and there are also errors in the log the problem compounds with the other issue I posted yesterday with publishing entries That does not happen if I wait for processing before querying on a side note you can check the status of a process through the API python response requests post f url uploads upload id action process headers Authorization f Bearer token while True time sleep 2 response requests get f url uploads upload id headers Authorization f Bearer token if not response json data process running break That s exactly the GET request I use to query the upload Without sleep you will likely corrupt your upload ah ok and this breaks with sleep if you sleep long enough to allow the processing to finish it works The problem is how long is long enough ah ok i see if this request is already raising issues then we have a problem 2 secs usually did it for me this never crashed so far ok so I m indeed enforcing a 2s sleep time in my code now Maybe that s enough and won t corrupt the upload even if the processing is not quite finished That would work for me I wonder if there s a way to make it more foolproof like slighlty delaying the initial POST response i think even 0 5s would be fine but yeah that could also be a feature request ok good to know Thanks Hi Tristan Bereau Which API endpoint are you using to upload the data originally The data should definitely not be mutated corrupted by a GET request so we have to somehow reproduce and fix this As a workaround you can keep on using sleep

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text: request ok good to know Thanks Hi Tristan Bereau Which API endpoint are you using to upload the data originally The data should definitely not be mutated corrupted by a GET request so we have to somehow reproduce and fix this As a workaround you can keep on using sleep Could you provide some script data that I could use as a starting point to reproduce this Hi Lauri Himanen It was the test API endpoint I ve tried to work out an MWE to share with you Interestingly I can t reproduce the issue with my MWE I can indeed upload an immediately query without corrupting the data There must be something intricate subtle that happens with my larger code and I m not sure what it is at the moment Let s close this issue for now because I can t reproduce it in a simple MWE and also because I can simply use a short sleep in my larger code to alleviate the problem Tristan Bereau Alright But do keep us posted if you can find a way to reproduce this Will do Thanks

title: HDF5 in NOMAD
text: Hi Alvin Noe Ladines Sandor Brockhauser Sebastian Brückner Sherjeel Shabih mentioned that we have now a new quantity with type HDF5 where we can point to a path in a HDF5 field That s good to know Concerning the same file format I would ask if we can think to a ReferenceQuantity specific for HDF5 files Together to this I would like to exchange on the visualization capabilities available in the ELN for Nexus files H5Web In this way we can have NOMAD archives referencing directly some data contained in a HDF5 file without a useless conversion to metainfo I have a use case for that The people making vapor deposition have an in situ reflectance monitoring for the growth rate They need to know the refractive index of his material at 800 degree celsius and they perform Ellipsometry at that temperature I plan to reference the ellipsometry Nexus file within the NOMAD archives Addictional integration of Nexus data and metainfo is to have this HDF5 type quantities to fetch some important metadata from the Nexus file and record it within the results section Another strategy is to have a normalize function picking some specific metadata from nexus file and writing them into title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: I m trying to describe some capabilities of various lab instruments in a nomad schema packages In our lab we would like for example to describe the range of values available for example of pressure in a process chamber The current solution is to design the quantities for the min and max value and then searching them in the research app In this way if an user will search for an equipment that works with an x value of pressure it can be searched by imposing the mimum values of the entries less or equal to the value desired and the maximum value greater or equal to x I hope that this could works but there is a more refined way to deal with this kind of problems in NOMAD Hampus Näsström This reminds me of the discussion about PhysicalProperty but I don t know what the current state is and where this can be found Can you say more Or who could we ask I think a min and max quantity sounds like a great solution here This reminds me also of the request for a ranges search widget we discussed at some point e G To find all datasets covering a certain range of values in one property e G An axis range this would be quite helpful currently I have a similar workaround with two search widgets for min and max values In a scatterplot it is possible If I understand what you are trying to do correctly Such filtering also works with histograms the issue is more that it is about ranges covered within a specific experiment e g the energy range scanned in an experiment The goal at least for my case would be to find all experiments which have data within a certain energy window where each experiment has a range of energies One can search for min and max of the energy range individually and combining two searches also achieve the required but not in one search widget This is what in my view such a range search widget should do i e search whether the selected range is inside the span min max Not sure if this is easily doable in elasticsearch though This is exactly the point In my case I have some instruments that should be findable by a research app Here I m trying to describe capabilities i e

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: search whether the selected range is inside the span min max Not sure if this is easily doable in elasticsearch though This is exactly the point In my case I have some instruments that should be findable by a research app Here I m trying to describe capabilities i e range of values of operative parameters allowed by the instrument In this way if I would operate some experiment with a certain value of energy current or any kind of parameters I can search all the instruments which contains that value in the range of allowed ones Clearly a single value or a range of values contained in the range described the exact value could be considered as an interval of zero length We can t index array values in elastic search as it would quickly fill up the index So for now you should go with the min max quantities which you can calculate from your array and populate in the normalize method Sure still having such a ranges widget based on these values would be good find me all datasets where a certain value or range falls within the span min max Yes but we will have to store the min and max values as quantities to do this

hash being used in the beta Can someone guide me please Florian Dobener is the expert on this You need to check the git hash in nomad staging under about See the marked part in the screenshot Just copy and insert it into the upper left search box when you re in nomad s gitlab rep Then you see the nomad version at this exact hash From there just navigate to your submodule you want to view the hash of the particular commit is right beside the submodule and when you click it you directly go to this specific hash in the repo This is the current version in staging https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR tree ff18c846bf71f89ce2f4d5644fce40e56e0db40b Thank you Florian Dobener Very clear and helpful Florian Dobener or someone else a short follow up Any idea how often the staging version is updated on average The reason for my confusion was that I expected it to be updated after more than a month but I can see now that that is not the case Afaik there is no fixed schedule I think it s updated on demand when new features are needed Probably Markus Scheidgen Lauri Himanen or Pepe Marquez can say more about this It s not a big deal I was just curious I asked this question a long time ago and got the same answer but I wasn t sure if 1 anything had changed or 2 we empirically had some idea how often this was the case even if we do not have a schedule for it That s also all the information I have I just checked whether I can see in gitlab when the staging ci was run This would give us an estimate but I wasn t really able to get a nice overview ok thanks for looking into it The issue of staging update frequency is raised more often lately Currently its only updated on demand as Florian Dobener mentioned Thus the staging deployment is somewhat stable and there are no nightly surprises However anything is possible and we can change that Joseph Rudzinski you can bring this up on the coordinators meeting

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: I can t build a docker image from this so not really suitable for running of oasis in K8s I m aware that not everything I m doing is supported custom nomad lab patches or K8s nomad installations However from my point of view this is a regression since in the past it was possible to have a single working tree with submodules install it locally run quick testing with bare bone linux while still being able to build a docker image when needed using the exact same environment Any advice would be welcomed Thanks for the detailed issue I can try to answer some of the technical stuff the physics stuff is a bit beyond my scope but Sarthak Kapoor might find the ML use case interesting and see if NOMAD actions might be a fit This is a work in progress but you can take a look at the docs and see if this could be something that might work here https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad docs pull 52 I cannot however customize nomad lab itself This is a limitation by design In order for the plugin system to function reliably and allow plugins to interoperate across different installations the base nomad lab must remain compatible and consistent In the past we encountered significant issues with customized nomad versions Certain plugins would work in one setup but break in another Potential solutions to revert back previous behaviour 1 In your nomad lab branch bring back the Dockerfile final stages from before and build it locally the previous way 2 Create a fork of nomad lab and release a package under a different name and make nomad distro template install this custom package instead of nomad lab Neither of the solutions are optimal but it could work Thanks I ll try the option 1 and report how it goes Hi Pavel Ondra ka your use case is quite interesting Do you plan to use NOMAD only for data aggregation or also to run training inference We are building a feature that allows triggering long running tasks in NOMAD independently from the entry processing and provides quite some freedom in what resources you wanna allocate for these tasks Would be happy to share more if you feel this will be useful I did not manage to get a working Dockerfile for latest nomad lab develop the last one I

the baseclasses instead of the schema classes Otherwise this would mean that I need to have an app for every schema at least if I want to narrow down the results by filters and if I want to use common widgets I know that for the most important JV values I could use the results But not all desired values are in there and also the same applies for some wet chemical processes so I would be happy if there is any other way Best regards Matthias I would be also interested in an option to have multiple closely related schemas in one app Hi hi We are working on two things Ideally we would be able to pick definitions in an app from a parent class Eventually the new guy will allow you to pick any quantity without having to have them registered in bthe app I think you can nput a list of schemas for title: Include new modifications of plugins in the Oasis Docker image
text: I am working on plugins for an Oasis with two separate repositories one for the plugin and one for the image distribution The two repositories were made using the templates nomad plugin template and nomad distribution template available in the FAIRmat Github My workflow is that I am developing my parser plugin locally and testing it with the nomad parse command to see if the information are extracted and I then push the modifications on the repo of the plugin To see the modification on my Oasis I regenerate the image by triggering the Actions workflow Build and publish docker images and then docker compose pull the new image as described in the README of the repo However when I introduce new commits in my plugin repo these changes are not integrated when I regenerate the Docker image In the past I used to completely delete the caches before regenerating the image so that the modifications in the plugin were then integrated in the Docker image But it seems that it is not working anymore The only option I found to integrate the new version of my plugin in my Docker image was to remove my plugin from the list commit the change let the new image be generated and then include back my plugin in the list with a new commit and use the new generated image That s why I had some questions about how the content of the plugins are handled when the image is generated and how it was possible to fluidify this workflow here and easily integrate the new changes of my plugin in my Oasis image Hi are you repositories public If so could you link them Are you using any version tags for your plugin If so I think simply increasing the minor version and updating the pyprojcet toml in your oasis repo should do the trick If not you could also specify a commit assuming you install it from a git repo since you can t really push new changes to PyPI without updating the version tag I m assuming that you now just have the plugin dependency in the Oasis pyproject toml pointing to the main branch or something like that The two are public yes here for the Docker image https github com Guillaume Gaisne New Oasis mechanism here for the parser plugin https github com Guillaume Gaisne nomad TXRM

title: Inheritance of annotations
text: Consider the following parent and child sections where the annotations are defined in the parent section py class Parent ArchiveSection m def Section a eln ELNAnnotation properties SectionProperties order quantity1 quantity2 quantity1 Quantity type np float64 quantity3 Quantity type np float64 class Child Parent m def Section quantity2 Quantity type np float64 It appears that when the child section is populated it does not contain the annotations from the parent Here s a screenshot of a similar situation where the child section NIRGain misses the section annotations defined in the parent SettingOverWavelengthRange Can we have an inheritance of the section annotations possibly conditioned on the child s annotation if there aren t any use the ones from the parent Currently I have a bunch of child sections of the same parent but I have to define the order of the quantities for each of them Hey I think there is a recent issue for this https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2049 it is about the order right yes this was never inherrited it needs to specified on the lowest level you can hower change the order of the quantities in the parent class it self sth like this quantity3 Quantity type np float64 quantity1 Quantity type np float64 i for my part copy it all in the child sections for years it is the same with hiding quantities looks like the things in m def Section are not carried over inheriting classes Ah interesting i just realized this The order of the quantities defined within a section is preserved standalone either child or parent Order becomes undefined when quantities from the child combine with the ones from the parent indeed since the annotations are also the state of the object it will be nicer if they are also inherited just like the quantities possibly tracking which annotations are overwritten or inherited Yes but this never works It also doesn t work to overwrite annotations of a quantity in a child other then restating the whole quantity It is one of the small things you first find confusing but start to love over time Yeah it seems that the best way to change an inherited quantity is to completely redefine it or do sth like py name Parent name m copy name description Changing the description this way the eln annotations of the quantity are also

title: Inheritance of annotations
text: find confusing but start to love over time Yeah it seems that the best way to change an inherited quantity is to completely redefine it or do sth like py name Parent name m copy name description Changing the description this way the eln annotations of the quantity are also carried over But not the a eln annotations to my knowledge If you change them like that Use this with care 2 month ago I fixed bugs removing this Yes it seems involved i was just trying to do this for m def py m def Parent m def m copy m def description something and unsurprisingly this creates issues when overwriting a quantity Copying the m def of parent section as is definitely not the right thing to do for preserving the annotations So i guess we stick with redefining the annotations for the children Yeah I think this is safer and not necessarily bad Can help with selfdocumentation quite a lot Yeah i agree What do you mean by self documentation though Ah that you don t need to inverse jump through the full hierarchy to understand the schema Imagine 6 class inheritances each changing a bit of a quantity Then debugging it Considering this I might be ok with how it is ah yeah in that case it does make sense to stick with the current way of working

title: Issue with UploadBundle (API)
text: I want to transfer an upload from an Oasis Server to my local deployment localhost via the API with the endpoint uploads bundle When I try this I get the following error ERROR API responded with status code 400 detail Failed to import bundle User does not exist Any ideas how to solve this I used an admin user so this shouldn t be the problem this sounds like an issue with the auth system you use locally and on the oasis i think the easiest is to replace in the zipl file all instances of the user id in the oasis with the user id you use for your local account That worked Thanks cool

title: Issue with docker for a local installation of NOMAD
text: Hello I have an issue with running docker for a local installation of nomad docker compose up fails with following message nomad elastic java lang IllegalStateException cannot downgrade a node from version 7 17 24 to version 7 17 1 nomad elastic exited with code 1 The same nomad installation run successfully before I tried to remove docker images and run docker again it pulled all the images anew but still failed with the same error Hello Are you trying docker compose up inside ops docker compose infrastructure folder It seems to run fine for me yes here is what i get in terminal The error indicates that you used a newer version of NOMAD and populated data then switched to an older branch which has the older Elasticsearch version This causes that error The brute force method of solving it is to clear all your local data with docker compose down volumes but note that this will delete all of the stored data in mongodb elastic etc of your installation yes this solved the issue Thank you

text: from custom https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR fork I assume you could change the source of your nomad lab dependency to point to either a git repo https docs astral sh uv concepts projects dependencies git or local project https docs astral sh uv concepts projects dependencies path In that case I can recommend cloning the nomad distro template repo locally and pointing nomad lab to a local project like Haoyu mentioned And then copy the local project into the Dockerfile and build it locally and run it via the local image that was built Generally speaking we discourage people using forks of nomad lab and instead recommend people to use plugins to customise their oasis There s a lot of additional complexity that comes with having to use a customised nomad lab package Thanks for the explanation

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text: When executing NOMAD it seems to misinterpret the unit min as millinch instead of minute this causes errors in the code I ve noticed that in the NOMAD Oasis the documentation uses docker compose instead of docker compose hey the correct unit term for minute is minute this is intended if you need milliinch it is min i am not sure why it is overloading pint here just tested with pint and there min resolves to minute Thank you for the clarification Astou THIAM this was an intentional error to show people how they can understand and fix mistakes This explanation is inlcuded in the docs here https nomad lab eu prod v1 oasis docs tutorial develop plugin html run the nomad cli Not sure what you mean with the docker compose vs the docker compose docker compose is the command that should be used to run the docker image as defined in the docker compose yaml config file https docs docker com reference cli docker compose older version of docker uses docker compose to run docker config files but recent versions use docker compose command Hi everyone How can we determine which plugin to use when reading a file and how do we navigate graphs on NOMAD HI Astou I ll try to provide some answers for the two questions 1 What do you mean by reading a file Do you mean when you upload a file to NOMAD What parser gets called is decide by the matching criteria The simplest criteria is to match on either the file name ending or the mime type of the file using the MatchingParser class You can find more information about this in the docs here https nomad lab eu prod v1 docs explanation processing html matching 2 Not sure exactly what you mean by graphs NOMAD doesn t have a graph backend but we do have an option of finding the back references between entries These are resolved in the GUI so to navigate in a graphical sense you can follow the references where they are place in the data section and the back references can all be found on the overview page As far as I know we have no API endpoint for querying across multiple references this would have to be done on the client side Maybe if you provide some more information on what you want to do

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text: and the back references can all be found on the overview page As far as I know we have no API endpoint for querying across multiple references this would have to be done on the client side Maybe if you provide some more information on what you want to do I could provide more information

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Currently the memory on many of the oases gets blocked over time and the only way to free it is to restart the containers Markus Scheidgen mentioned to me at some point that this is due to the workers being reused so if there is some memory leak in them they block the memory indefinitely He did mention that there was a way to limit the reuse and or number of workers somehow to mitigate this As it s Markus last day today I guess he won t be able to answer but maybe Lauri Himanen has an idea who could Thanks Probably this https docs celeryq dev en stable userguide workers html max memory per child setting Cool Any idea what could be a reasonable value for this How many workers do we create I guess that s also configurable Ideally the number of workers times the max memory would be less than some fraction of the system memory I guess By default it s the number of cores https docs celeryq dev en stable userguide workers html concurrency Great thanks python c import multiprocessing print multiprocessing cpu count should give you the right number I guess the max memory per child has a similar effdect But there is another important setting This is the one i was mentioning once This one specifically mentiones the use case of memory leaks https docs celeryq dev en latest userguide workers html max tasks per child setting Basically allows you to restart a worker process based on the number of tasks it has run Eg restart a worker every 100 archives Ah right Now I remember Thank you Do we have default values for this stored somewhere There is nothing on the docker compose but the k8s version of nomad uses 128 Its in the default values yaml of the helm chart in the distro project Okay great It would possibly make sense to have a smaller default for the distro template ahmed ilyas as a lot of people start out with not so much system memory Yeah i think that makes sense I am in a similar situation that I want to limit the number of workers for our oasis as the machine has very many cores Where and how can I configure this We set the limit on number of tasks https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad distro template pull
