title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text: After following the last tutorial on the installation of an Oasis using the Github repo to create a Docker image of an Oasis with plugin code installed I am trying to make it work on my machine by developing a parser for the DAMASK code Since I am not familiar with the coding convention of NOMAD I make a lot of changes to test the output visible on the Oasis I am creating and so it means that following the steps of the tutorial once I make a modification on the parser code I generate a new Docker image that I want to test So each time I want to test my new Oasis image I do sudo rm r nomad oasis with nomad oasis being the folder containing the docker compose yaml file and the configs folder and I also delete the Docker image with docker rmi id of the image and then I curl the new image and proceed to the steps of the installation However when I open the Oasis in my browser I can see the entries but when I click on one of them each of them I have this message Unexpected error object Object 500 Please try again and let us know if this error keeps happening And the Files panel says ERROR Could not initialize view This happens whether the entries are published or just uploaded so for now it means that I can t publish any dataset because when I will install a new modified image I won t be able to properly access the entry and so I have to republish them Is this a normal behaviour or am I doing something wrong when I am trying to visualize the modifications I made on my image by deleting the repo and the Docker image systematically Hi Guillaume You should not need to delete anything when updating the image Taken directly from the image README Whenever you update your image you need to shut down NOMAD using docker compose down and then repeat steps 5 and 6 above With steps 5 and 6 being 5 Pull the images specified in the docker compose yaml retrieved from the nomad oasis zip Note that the image needs to be public or you need to provide a PAT see Important note above docker compose pull 6 And run it with docker compose in detached detach

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text: 6 being 5 Pull the images specified in the docker compose yaml retrieved from the nomad oasis zip Note that the image needs to be public or you need to provide a PAT see Important note above docker compose pull 6 And run it with docker compose in detached detach or d mode docker compose up d When you do the delete command from above you are deleting the files folder which contains all of the entries so that is why NOMAD cannot find them Ok thanks Guess I haven t checked the image README correctly So if the files folder has been deleted how is it that the entries in the Explore page are still showing up I mean I can still see the entries but if I go the their Entry Page there nothing is displayed That s a bit above my knowledge of the NOMAD backend but my guess would be that the Mongo DB index somehow survived in it s own docker image while the files it s referencing to were deleted Markus Scheidgen might be able to tell you more It is possible see the entries and have the files deleted Depending on the view the GUI shows information from mongodb or from elasticsearch With our default docker compose based install the mongo and elastic databases are put into a docker volume If you use the same docker installation but removed all the files the docker volumes will still be there If you want to delete everything you also need to delete the docker volumes By default the volumes are called nomad oasis elastic nomad oasis mongo and nomad oasis rabbitmq With docker volume rm you can delete volumes If you routinely want to wipe all data in your NOMAD installation you can also use the nomad cli from within one of the containers docker exec ti nomad oasis app bash nomad admin reset i am really sure If you just want to update your Oasis you should not delete anything All uploaded data lives in the volumes folder and will be lost if you delete it Just follow what Hampus Näsström was writing down pull up d again

errors or warnings in the logs With Keycloak 24 everything still worked I have attached a minimal example based on https nomad lab eu prod v1 docs assets nomad oasis with keycloak zip Here are the title: Logout button does not logout north
text: If I open a NORTH container with one user name in a browser then logout from Nomad login with another user in the same browser and try to open any NORTH container it fails with 403 Forbidden You do not have permission to access Server at because the previous user does not get logged off from the jupyter hub It can be solved by manually logout from the jupyter hub but ideally the Nomad logout process should do this Occurs both on the central nomad and a nomad oasis installation Adam Fekete Can you change this This is a tricky question In keycloak each client application service works separately even they belong to the same realms with admin priviledges on keycloak we can logout from all section for a given user but doing as a user it is not obvious https w0l1d medium com implementing global logout in keycloak a comprehensive guide c6f22d4034bd I can find single sign out in the list of feature of keycloak https www keycloak org docs 25 0 6 server admin index html but I do not find any other documentation about the details or how we should set it up So currently you also have to log in to NOMAD and NORTH independently right because the two clients belongs to the same realm you only need to login one thanks to the single sign in method as far as i know the when you logout from one client you also remove the session token and you can login with another user from the another client application point of view the old session token still valid and usable but it belong to another account Ok so we would have to invalidate the session token so that the second app does a reauthentification Could there be cases where people would like to stay logged into NORTH as user1 regardless of what they do with the auth at NOMAD Does logging out kill your notebooks Logging out does not stop any container so I don t see an issue with this I think it should be possible to issue a logout command on the jupyter hub side but I don t understand well how the session handling is working

title: Menage palette colours in an image generated in a NeXuS file
text: Hello I m building a NeXus file for a SEM measurement I was wondering if it is possible to mantain the orginal palette because I m not able to reproduce the grey scale of the orginal image Probably this is linked to the way the image is automatically generated by nomad after processing the arary given There exist a way to define the palette desired or if not already present could be included Hi Trogg the question is essentially how to reproduce a specific rendering Please share some context if the dataset is already in nomad could you share the view here if not which specific SEM measurement is it You might know that pynxtools em is supporting out of the box already a majority of tiff and several proprietary hdf5 based cases from all the big tech em partners so that these developments can be used latest updates for pynxtools em are provided by v0 4 0 which will be included in the 1 3 17 nomad release representing contrast in two different greyscale images exactly requires the same gamma curve I have solved it From nomad in the visualizer of the image it is possible to choose the palette and also perform other transformation on the image selecting ranges and other specification

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
text: FAIRmat internal areaD Is there any existing plans to have something like the Metainfo Browser that can be launched directly from a plugin Nathan Daelman and I were discussing this morning and think this would fullfill a long time desire to be able to browse domain specific Metainfo structures The normal metainfo browser should show plugins as a source when installed But I guess you want something where you don t have a nomad installation and just get a browser e g nomad admin run metainfo browser yeah something like that The main case we had in mind is that a user wants to understand the schema structure that is included within a plugin e g from the standard simulation schema used in the central NOMAD this is linked to how we should add the new schemas that we are developing to the corresponding plugin docs which is why the question was initially raised But this then generalizes to a tool where anyone can visualize the schema that they are building which is nice In the past we were always talking about how to point users to the sections quantities of the metainfo that are actually relevant to their domain With the move to schema plugins this is now straightforward with such a tool For install plugins you can use the normal metainfo browser e g here is the nomad measurments on the example oasis https nomad lab eu prod v1 oasis gui analyze metainfo nomad measurements Maybe we can improve the metainfo browser but there is no plan for an external tool service ok thanks a lot I will have a look I guess eventually we should hightlight the fact that this comes from plugins better I think we should at least provide a method for printing out all the inherited quantities and subsection of a section I raised this in a comment somewhere earlier Currently there is a pretty print method but when we use multiple inheritance it quickly becomes of limited use Technically one would need to create a smaller version of the UI with a separate build that can then be loaded from a small dev server run from the command line Not impossible to do but also quit some work Hampus Näsström idea could be archives with a CLI command that used m to dict parameters to also include the derived all quantities Maybe you

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins

title: Migrating to new plugin mechanism - Problem with Parser
text: Hey I have a problem when migrating data from my old Oasis old locally stored plugin mechanism to my new Oasis new plugin mechanism with entrypoints I transferred an upload via the API using upload bundle and get the error Processing failed AssertionError Invalid entry metadata parser UMR I guess the reason is that I changed the name of my parser in the new plugin to nomad perolab umr parsers cicci txt parser entry point For schemas there is the chance to give alias names Is there something similar for parsers I hope you understand my problem Thanks Note sure if this works for parser packages too Could you give it a try and report back here https nomad lab eu prod v1 staging docs howto plugins schema packages html schema package aliases ahmed ilyas FYI Hi Aaron Schüller Ruhl There is indeed aliases tag for the parsers and most likely that s why it is failing Here I am attaching an example configuration for a parser python from nomad config models plugins import ParserEntryPoint class TestEntryPoint ParserEntryPoint def load self from path to test parser import TestParser return TestParser self dict test parser entry point TestEntryPoint name parsers test aliases parsers test parsers test parser code name test code homepage https www test net description test pasrer mainfile mime re r test mainfile name re r test json You need to use the aliases and set it to your old parser name this should fix it Perfect that worked for me Thanks

title: Modify the archive structure?
text: I m trying to describe some material properties in a schema For example it is mandatory to describe stress properties of the material etching rates for different techniques and probably others in the future I was wondering if could be possible to modify the properties section in results I think this should be the best option but I don t know if as an user I m allowed to do it Another possible solution is to add a section to the EntryArchive i e adding a field to data metadata and results but I don t know how it is possible If all these solutions are wrong I will create a subsection in data with the Section ad hoc for properties needed Please put them in the data section The results section is a protected section that can only be extended in the NOMAD source code in the nomad FAIR repository With the latest updates you should be able to search and use the quantities in the data section in the same way as those from results so the need for the results section is slowly disappearing Thanks for the explanations Anyway could you expain more about the last point What does it mean that the latest updates allows to search in a different way the quantities You can add them as searchable quantities in apps https nomad lab eu prod v1 staging docs howto plugins apps html loading quantity definitions into an app Ok of course I know this I misunderstood thinking that a new reference to quantities was added Thanks for all

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: I was presenting FAIR data FAIRmat and NOMAD yesterday and I got a lot of questions about how to modify remove data that has already been uploaded Or the possibility to add more comments or additional analysis to an entry Because I m mostly focused on formatting the data at the moment I don t have a lot of experience with data curation So here are for example a few more practical questions I was asked Imagine you have a setup that directly outputs data to your NOMAD OASIS can you then modify delete annotate the data sets add some field to it etc Imagine you have an already published experimental dataset and you discover a calibration error Can you correct it And if yes is there some kind of versioning so that maybe people having used this dataset before see that what is in now is different from what they have used If you change affiliation can you update it in your datasets So yes the question would be what is possible and until which level of sharing like how is the answer when the data is unloaded but not shared how is it once it s shared and how is it once it has a DOI for example Hi I just want to comment on the data curation part Data curation means that there is some control on the quality of the entries that are being generated but NOMAD is not like that In the central service we accept any kind of data Now an Oasis is a different thing you can decide how to handle this Now Imagine you have a setup that directly outputs data to your NOMAD OASIS can you then modify delete annotate the data sets add some field to it etc Being admin of your oasis you can essentially re run re process entries at will https nomad lab eu prod v1 staging docs howto oasis admin html You could have a script running at a different moment in time to run your entries to modify delete annotate fields This can be done creating and maintaining a parser plugin This is also how the central NOMAD works we have some parsers that change over time and we reprocess sometimes some of the entries present in the database either to fix bugs to populate new fields or change some Imagine you have an already

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: creating and maintaining a parser plugin This is also how the central NOMAD works we have some parsers that change over time and we reprocess sometimes some of the entries present in the database either to fix bugs to populate new fields or change some Imagine you have an already published experimental dataset and you discover a calibration error Can you correct it And if yes is there some kind of versioning so that maybe people having used this dataset before see that what is in now is different from what they have used My previous comment answers the first part of the question Sadly for the versioning part no we don t control the version of an entry in the database So once you reprocess an entry the information is overwritten and we don t keep any registry of the previous entries I think this is a feature request Maybe Lauri Himanen can tell you more but for sure we didn t start working on it If you change affiliation can you update it in your datasets There is another issue regarding affiliation but I don t know whether it is updated in the datasets Good question maybe Sascha Klawohn knows Hi Julien KAUST Currently we only have fairly primitive ways to update data If your data is not published you can change it at any time but any old data will be overwritten If your data is published the raw data becomes immutable and you cannot change it If you want to correct something you will need to make a new upload dataset with the corrected data This process is similar to scientific articles errata can be added but the original publication cannot be changed Note that any derived data e g data produced by a parser can change and often does since we improve the parsers fairly regularly Author affiliation can be changed since it is not tied to an upload dataset We are currently having some problems with the form that allows you to do it this is discussed here https discord com channels REDACTED PHONE REDACTED PHONE threads REDACTED PHONE

workflows 10 at a time check for successful processing then publish and move on to the next ten This at least is how I have approached it with a postdoc I am working with I could send a script later During this process I would store the entry and upload IDs in whatever organization you need to later make the workflows or datasets these you can perform after the entries are published Do you think this could work for you Can you give me an overarching picture of your org what you would like to group into workflows or datasets etc I am on a train now but if you send me the relevant info I can share some ideas and scripts with you later I would still like to test some representative entries from your data I think I have them now but I may ask again later and sorry for the difficulty We will try to work on a better solution

where is it getting stuck Joseph Rudzinski do you have somone from Area C that could take on this task Luis Walter what is your smallest upload that does not get processed And how large is it roughly in terms of disk space I will DM you to discuss Lauri Himanen The smallest upload I have which got stuck after processing 8 files is QeFGvhPHSgSdYtxbbuuj4A I have an idea what could cause the problem I uploaded the files of a single simulation run individually with the GROMACS log file first which is the parsing entry point I noticed that all files get processed basically immediately except for the edr file This file contains energy pressure temperature etc during the simulation In my test case the file contains only 170 values per property timesteps Nevertheless it takes approximately 5 minutes to parse this file If I upload the simulation files without the edr file the upload finishes the processing step almost immediately This is an example simulation data With the edr file the processing takes really long 7 minutes without it the processing finishes almost immediately however it results in a FAILED state because of the missing edr file Luis Walter Alvin Noe Ladines looked into your uploading problems and found that there were some issues with efficiency and storage in our xvg parser within the Gromacs parser He has addressed these and it appears the issues with your sticking uploads are resolved These changes are already present in the develop deployment It appears then that you can go back to your original simulation organization If you have further issues of course let us know Thanks to Lauri Himanen and Alvin Noe Ladines for your timely assistance with this Luis Walter Note that the fixes are enabled only in our develop deployment which contains nightly updates for our development purposes So if you want to reprocess something yourself you need to go to http nomad lab eu prod v1 develop gui Also let me

title: Newest nomad-lab release not published on PyPI
text: When starting to use the nomad lab with the documentation we noticed that the version on PyPI is 1 2 1 whereas the latest release on the GitHub is 1 3 3 We noticed this because the examples in the documentation do not work with the PyPI version but the documentation explicitly states to install nomad lab via pip You are right You can still use pip you just have to add index url https gitlab mpcdf mpg de api v4 projects 2187 packages pypi simple In some Tutorials this is explicitly mentioned e g Tutorial 13 pip install upgrade pip pip install e dev index url https gitlab mpcdf mpg de api v4 projects 2187 packages pypi simple Note Until we have an official pypi NOMAD release with the latest NOMAD version make sure to include NOMAD s internal package registry e g via index url The latest pypi package available today is version 1 2 2 and it misses some updates functional to this tutorial In the future when a newer release of nomad lab will be available 1 2 2 you can omit the index url Thanks for the tip maybe this should be added to the official documentation at https nomad lab eu prod v1 docs howto programmatic pythonlib html

title: Nexus Search very slow for larger entry numbers
text: I have noticed that the searching for nexus quantities is really slow once there are a sizable number or results 100 200 and a number of search widgets are on the dashboard I ve seen times up to 40 s or more to update a search for this yet not so large number of results I ve located the code that takes most of the time to the section here https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob jumpToReference nomad search py L510 In particular this line seems to be relevant https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob jumpToReference nomad search py L525 running this line once takes about 0 5 ms and there are 400 search quantities per entry making 200 ms per entry for a total of 188 entries in my example this is 40 s Lauri Himanen Sandor Brockhauser any idea on how to improve this Changed the channel name Nexus Search very slow for larger entry numbers How much RAM do you have on your server Maybe anecdotal but we observed a considerable speedup when we increased the memory on one Oasis This test installation only has 8 GByte ram indeed not a lot I can check if it goes to swap during searches From profiling the app process it appears that there are tens to hundreds of miillion calls to these functions Oh 8 GB is definitely not a lot I think it might not only be if it goes to the swap or not but simply how much gets allocated to elastic search in the first place But here Lauri Himanen could probably give some more details I would definitely increase the RAM especially if you are running NORTH containers I always recommend 16 GB minumum and 32 if you plan to have concurrent use of NORTH As far as I understand it s not an issue with elasticsearch but whats taking so long is handling the results But I can try to test on a different machine It appears mostly to be an issue for scatter plots as here every entry creates one search result For Histograms or terms its less of an issue because the results are already aggregated Thanks Laurenz Rettig for this analysis this is really helpful I don t think this is an issue with hardware I think that we can optimize this part

title: Nexus Search very slow for larger entry numbers
text: here every entry creates one search result For Histograms or terms its less of an issue because the results are already aggregated Thanks Laurenz Rettig for this analysis this is really helpful I don t think this is an issue with hardware I think that we can optimize this part you pointed out especially in the case of scatter plots The scatter plots only typically require 2 3 search quantities so I hope we can skip most of the other search quantities in that loop just by looking at the required fields that are already sent in the request Let me create an issue out of this and I see what we can do That makes sense that can probably also speed up other widgets as for histograms terms you even only need one search quantity no In the case of histogram terms the code is not even looking at the search quantity values directly but we instead use the internal aggregation calls from ES So these widgets don t have the same performance problem This indeed seems to help a lot I drafted this solution here as a starting point https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 2408 I also saw that the searched quantities are in principle contained in doc type quantities however with a different schema according to the application definition e g pynxtools nomad schema Mpes rather than pynxtools nomad schema Root I assume this could then only be used by searching just within one appdef right With this fix scatter plots are actually updating faster now than histograms or terms I also noticed that scatter plots don t show data if two items are selected within one Terms widget OR search while the histograms do show the data This was however already the behavior without the fix Hi Laurenz Rettig Thanks a lot for the MR I also started on this this morning as I did not saw this message before I m roughly doing the same things you did but added a bit better support for handling include exclude patterns If you have the time could you test out my branch here https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 2408 and see if it also fixes the performance problem for you Lauri Himanen this does not quite work histogram data are missing I provided some context

title: Nexus Search very slow for larger entry numbers
text: If you have the time could you test out my branch here https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 2408 and see if it also fixes the performance problem for you Lauri Himanen this does not quite work histogram data are missing I provided some context You mean scatter plot data is missing I will have a look yes exactly the results only contain the entry ids as the paths do not match the root schema Ok I think I know the reason Laurenz Rettig I updated the MR with some new commits and comments This seems to work now I added some more context regarding the performance issue also with the other searches The respective slow code is only called if this does not match quantity doc type quantities get id I e I believe for unregistered search quantities

title: NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method
text: When running nomad installation with app worker the m context is an instance of the class nomad datamodel context ServerContext which has a well defined raw path exists method But while running pytests which uses the nomad as a package and not the local installation m context is an instance of class nomad datamodel context Context in which raw path exists is not implemented Because of this difference the pytests fail during the plugin testing when using archive m context raw path exists file path Can we harmonize these two methods for the classes ServerContext and Context Clarification when using pytest m context is of class nomad datamodel context ClientContext which inherits nomad datamode context Context and as the child doesn t have its own raw path exists method it defaults to the one from the parent which is not implemented Something like this could resolve it https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR commit 2e8d24cd139cef1a9b9b7244c25c00b4333f28f1 I guess we only implemented the Context interface based on what was necessary The ServerContext is the more complete one as this is used all the time in the app The ClientContext is only used by well clients E g when you do the nomad parse command or the ArchiveQuery instantiates metainfo object froms the retrieved data For the first case nomad parse you commit seems ok For the second one it does not hurt but it also does not help as the raw data is most likely not locally available I guess it complements the existing raw functions Feel free to create an MR and merge this I agree At least this resolves an error I face during a pytest for plugin that uses m context raw path exists method I will go ahead and make a MR for this Thanks

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: We are currently trying to upload our data backlog to our local Oasis The files are all converted to the NeXus format and correctly parsed However after rough 100 200 uploaded files the server PC freezes and needs to be restarted manually We noticed that during uploading the swap memory size gradually increases until it is full and the server freezes This only happens when uploading nexus files and happens also with the example ellipsometry nxs file when uploaded multiple times under different names Has anybody seen something similar or advice on how to handle this Dear Johannes Given that you use a local oasis providing a little more context would be good to enable at all a search for answers to your observations These are guiding questions Were all files uploaded into one upload or multiple at once how API call drag and drop Was the total data volume in GB in the order of the amount of main memory that was made available to the container or is available physically left on the server your message suggests you hit main memory limits and end up swapping combined with a server with a spinning disk and yay Which version of Oasis and plugins are you using The ellipsometry example as it is shipped is tiny provided it was not modified with own data there might be parsing errors in which case the logs record these issues but that should not make the system completely unresponsive Similar uploads might get stuck sometimes but still the operation system remains operational so in the case of the ellipsometry example I guess you made an empty upload and dragged and dropped the NeXus file Did this nevertheless then sent the server into frozen state Dear Markus thank you for your response i will try to provide a more detailed account of our setup and tests I work with API calls made with a python script and the request package I tried both creating an upload per file as well as using a PUT request to add files to an existing upload both with the same result I always make sure a file is fully processed before uploading the next file either via checking the upload status in a 3s loop as suggested in the docs or the wait for processing parameter in the PUT request The nxs files in question are converted from

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: the same result I always make sure a file is fully processed before uploading the next file either via checking the upload status in a 3s loop as suggested in the docs or the wait for processing parameter in the PUT request The nxs files in question are converted from one of our laboratory instruments and all have 1 3MB Even 200 such are much smaller than the 8GB RAM and 4GB swap memory we work with on the server We do hit the main memory limit before utilizing the swap memory but it never resets and eventually no swap memory is left and the freeze happens At this point we were not able to do anything but manually restart the computer via the power button it does not respond to anything We are running Oasis version 1 3 13 and pynxtools version 0 9 3 I did all my testing via python script and requests I can test drag and drop tomorrow and get back to you So far I used drag and drop only to check whether the parser is running successful or not Our goal is to build an automated data pipline from the instrument s to our Oasis After a freeze an upload is usually stuck in the processing state which i then reset via the admin console The server is responsive in this state I tried to use the ellipsometry example to exclude problems with our NeXus files although the parser always matches and runs successfully for our files I now see that there are a bunch warnings 40 in the logs after the parser has run both for our nexus files as well as for the ellipsometry file WARNING pynxtools nomad entrypoints nexus parser error while setting field or attribute However no errors Sounds very like some memory leak problem in the handling of hdf5 files Johannes Dallmann thank you very much for your detailed report of your observations these mirror also some of our experiences where we faced issues with the memory consumption of the current implementation for the NeXus HDF5 parsing and normalization pipeline I created an issue on our board for this to not forget about it Mind thought that the root cause can range from sth trivial to fix to something which may demand time consuming memory profiling a non trivial multi threaded application Which CPU is this server powered

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: normalization pipeline I created an issue on our board for this to not forget about it Mind thought that the root cause can range from sth trivial to fix to something which may demand time consuming memory profiling a non trivial multi threaded application Which CPU is this server powered by It is good to know that you observe this relevant high mem consumption even when you upload via API calls only one file at a time i e sequential uploads Mind though that 8GB in total is not much specifically if 4GB is reserved here for swap that seems a quite fraction offered for swap Nevertheless your expectation is right ideally processing an a few MB file should NOT demand allocating dozens of GBs I assume that you run this OASIS then essentially more like local development installation right Meaning there is no container orchestrator e g k8s that would kick the container in which the NOMAD worker runs from the system if the container takes too much memory like what is the case on the production NOMAD that runs on servers Cuz typically the container scheduler should protect the OS from having one container attempting to drain that much all memory It is rather this last bit which in my opinion makes the issue more severe than just an issue like well NOMAD may demand much main memory the system should never ideally become that frozen Hi Markus Kuehbach I m running the development Nomad Oasis together with Johannes Thanks for your really fast help so far Our CPU on the development system is AMD Phenom tm II X6 1055T Processor 64 bit 6 cores 3GHz freq per core Initially we had 1GB of swap accompanying our RAM We increased just to see if that would allow the uploads to be stable for longer which didn t really work I was looking into ways to limit docker memory allocation size but am still relatively new to it Our docker images have a max Memory allocation of 2GiB according to the docker service Is there an easy way to keep the docker images from bleeding the memory leak I will have a look towards the orchastrators in the meantime first this is the a reference for such questions https docs docker com engine containers resource constraints understand the risks of running out of memory https jasebell medium com a

title: Overwriting subsections with quantities not possible?
text: Is it intended that MetaInfo does not allow to overwrite a SubSection with a Quantity For example py class MyProcess Process steps Quantity type str description The steps of the process a eln ELNAnnotation component ELNComponentEnum StringEditQuantity repeats True raises an nomad metainfo metainfo MetainfoError Cannot inherit from different property types since in the basesections Process has already a repeating SubSection called that way py class Process Activity steps SubSection section def ProcessStep description An ordered list of all the dependant steps that make up this activity repeats True I have not tested it the other way around yet The other way around as well Yes that is very much intended

title: Parsing Polarizabilities and Dipole Moments
text: Hi I have simulations run with the code FHI AIMS They are Molecular Dynamics and Single Point calculations The Molecular Dynamics calculations include the Total Dipole Moment which is not parsed in the data processing The Single Point calculations calculate polarizabilities But when uploaded the polarizability tensor is also not parsed I would like to ask the developers to include the parsing of the dipole moment and the polarizabilites They are expensive calculations and necessary for the calculation of anharmonic IR and Raman spectra Let me know if you need further information or any help I can provide Thanks Hi Basillicus Adding support for these quantities should be no problem however it may take some time as we are currently overhauling NOMAD s simulation schema and parsers My team and I will discuss this and get back to you by early next week I expect You mentioned also a problem with timing out during processing Would you be able to add me as a reviewer to that upload Or is the upload stuck in processing Thanks Joseph I ll keep an eye on this feature request next week Re the upload issue I added you as a reviewer I may open a new issue for that problem to keep things organised if that is OK yes sure Basillicus Could you paste the link of whatever entries that you share with me here in the chat only people with access will be able to open them I have multiple accounts with many shared entries so it is not easy for me to find otherwise Also if this entry that you share is not an example with polarizabilities and dipole moments could you also share one of those so that I can look into extending our support Thanks Hi Joseph Rudzinski thanks for looking into it The links I added you as reviewer are 1 For the Time Limit Exceeded error https nomad lab eu prod v1 gui user uploads upload id 8X5VOIwgQFKJSWIAJzsvOw I am not sure if the 27 GB zip file I uploaded is still there after the Time Limit Error I can not see it but it may just be not accessible for me 2 I uploaded a subset with only 50 entries as a test that contains polarizabilities as Single Point calculations and Dipole Moments in the Molecular Dynamics runs The link to the upload entry is https

title: Permission denied when creating new Jupyter Notebook in uploads folder
text: ahmed ilyas Do you have an idea what could be the problem here This is similar to the issue Hampus Näsström also had I m not sure what s causing this though Aaron Schüller Ruhl Looking at your description you might be accidentally doing the wrong thing you need to first navigate inside the upload folder then right click and press New Notebook this will create it inside the folder The way you described it will try to create it in the uploads folder which is not permitted A Jupyter user does not have write priviledges for that Sorry I misread I think you are doing the correct thing Looks very much like a file permission issue to me I have verified that e g in our staging deployment you can create these notebooks inside an upload I think I know what the problem is It is indeed a permission issue The owner of the volumes folder is in my case the user root not user with uid 1000 I made 1000 the owner and this worked But the problem now is when I create new uploads the owner of those uploads and the subdirectories of volumes respectively is again the user root So I somehow need to find a way how the user with uid 1000 creates the folders or I need to map my root user to the docker user 988 in the docker compose file What would you say is the better way Then it looks like your Jupyter image is somehow incorrectly using the root user Aaron Schüller Ruhl Is your Oasis based on nomad distro template https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad distro template Adam Fekete I see in our Dockerfile https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad distro template blob 39509dfe85640b8a1b25923a040668a8d92cdb98 Dockerfile L203 that the user is set to NB UID in the Jupyter image Does this default to 1000

title: Pint and pydantic update
text: I would like to know about the status of the pint and pydantic update I could only find this open issue https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2168 And some time ago the request for pint 0 24 and pydantic 2 here on discord https discord com channels REDACTED PHONE REDACTED PHONE REDACTED PHONE Are there any plans if when these changes will come Hi Carla Terboven We do have issues for both of these libraries https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2142 and https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2168 The main problem is that both updates require quite a bit of work as in the case of Pydantic we need to update our code to use the new interface and in the case of Pint we would need to change several of our parsers due to this issue https github com hgrecco pint issues 1809 So I cannot promise that we will do this migration very urgently as there are not yet that many plugins that would require this migration Ok I see thank you very much for the update Lauri Himanen I would be very happy if you could find the time for the issue at some point Carla Terboven Update here we have now updated to Pydantic 2 0 0 in nomad v1 3 15 and we will try to get the update for Pint into v1 3 16 which will should be released late April Wow thank you very much for the effort

title: PlotSection for Static Image
text: Hi NOMAD Team I asked myself if there s any built in functionality to plot show an image such as tif as these are the results of SEM measurements https en wikipedia org wiki Scanning electron microscope Therefore I came up to abuse plotly to create a plot section and display the image as static background https plotly com python images zoom on static images python class MeasurementSEM ELNMeasurement PlotSection ArchiveSection Class for handling measurement of SEM m def Section label Measurement SEM a eln overview True data file Quantity type str description A reference to an uploaded tif produced by the SEM instrument a browser adaptor RawFileAdaptor a eln component FileEditQuantity def normalize self archive EntryArchive logger BoundLogger The normalize function of the MeasurementSEM section try Check if any file is provided if self data file Check if the file has the correct extension if not self data file endswith tif if not self data file endswith tiff raise DataFileError f The file self data file must have a tif or tiff extension python continued normalized fct Otherwise parse the file as binary with archive m context raw file self data file rb as imagefile with Image open imagefile as img Get the size of the image img width img height img size Convert the image to RGB necessary for JPEG img img convert RGB Create a BytesIO object to hold the image data buffered io BytesIO Save the image to the BytesIO object in JPEG format img save buffered format JPEG Get the byte data img str base64 b64encode buffered getvalue decode utf 8 Create the URI image string uri f data image jpeg base64 img str python see https plotly com python images zoom on static images fig go Figure scale factor 1 0 fig add trace go Scatter x 0 img width scale factor y 0 img height scale factor mode markers marker opacity 0 Configure axes fig update xaxes visible False range 0 img width scale factor fig update yaxes visible False range 0 img height scale factor the scaleanchor attribute ensures that the aspect ratio stays constant scaleanchor x Add image fig add layout image dict x 0 sizex img width scale factor y img height scale factor sizey img height scale factor xref x yref y opacity 1 0 layer below sizing stretch source uri python Configure other layout fig update layout width

title: Plotly updated to version 6.0.0 is now breaking the nomad plotly figures
text: In version 6 0 0 plotly json dump uses a binary represenation of the data this leads to the data not being represented correctly see below before and after for now we downgrade to 6 mohammad nakhaee FYI Sarthak Kapoor Micha have you tried using json loads plot to json instead of plot to plotly json This fixed an earlier issue I had so this issue is fixed by downgrading Okay I just know that plotly has two methods for exporting to json and the to plotly json one was not working for some more complicated plots for me ok the export to json works fine just the data representation was changed i suspect that the python plotly was updated and the javascript plotly is not able to now display the data but good to know we use indeed to plotly json

text: Looking at the attached file if I instantiate MyClassOne the plot made with plotly express appears If I instantiate MyClassTwo and I fill in the data in the subsection my class one the plot appears only in the root but the plotly express does not show up anymore Maybe mohammad nakhaee or Lauri Himanen have some hint Hi Andrea Mohammad is on vacation sot let me try to answer When you say that MyClassOne is not appearing could you tell me where do you not see it You don t see a plot when you go to the archive browser and look at data my class one 0 Yes sorry I ll show better with some screenshots If I instantiate an entry with MyClassOne the plot trace is appearing If I instantiate an entry with MyClassTwo the plot frame is there but no trace is shown Several things are mixed up here I have in the same archive a plotly express and a native nomad plot in one case the class is the root in the other I nest the class inside another one In principle none of these things should affect the plotting though Alright now I see I will have a look at this solved here https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 2286 note 299421

title: Plugin Loaded twice
text: As you see from the screenshot we Sebastian Brückner Hampus Näsström Sarthak Kapoor noticed that when loading a plugin we get doubled lines in the create function This is the plugin https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad measurements And this is the nomad yaml plugins include nomad measurements general schema nomad measurements xrd schema nomad measurements xrd parser nomad measurements transmission schema nomad measurements transmission parser We even tried to remove the schema packages but the double line remains as well Any ideas on that Lauri Himanen I can try to investigate this You are seeing when using the main branch of nomad measurements Ok I think I figured it out The aliasing mechanism is causing this duplication This issue is being tracked in 2122 https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2122 Should be fairly easy to solve I keep you updated There is now a MR https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 2087 that fixes the duplication of the schemas in the entry menu Could the three of you Sarthak Kapoor Sebastian Brückner Hampus Näsström coordinate to test this Now that I m removing the duplication of the metainfo I m no longer sure if entries that use the old alias will be displayed properly Maybe you could test this branch with entries that use both old and new m defs sure on it It works well Our schemas are not duplicated anymore If I add an entry with the old m def it is processed successfully and the m def is overwritten with the new one btw some of the schemas coming from nomad are still being duplicated like the workflow ones I guess this is workflow vs workflow2 cool then the fix looks good thanks Lauri Himanen Alright I proceed with the merge

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text: Hi NOMAD Team thanks for the great tutorial 13 on plugins https www youtube com watch v c99XEC6GPfE and how to create them During plugin development I stumbled about a question and maybe somebody knows a solution for that How to self reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity As use case say you have an ELNSample which holds maybe a list of other ELNSamples is was derived from something like data linage sample history I used the following exemplary code class CRCSample ElnWithFormulaBaseSection ReadableIdentifiers EntryData ArchiveSection m def Section Sample reference from collaboration Quantity type CRCSample description If sample is received by collaboration then reference it here a eln component ReferenceEditQuantity shape and more but the line with type CRCSample throws a passing error if I reference another class everything parses If they are defined in the same python file you can give the type as a string However I would not recommend using the ElnWithFormulaBaseSection as it is being depricated But this is how you would do it py class CRCSample CompositeSystem EntryData m def Section sample reference from collaboration Quantity type CRCSample description If sample is received by collaboration then reference it here a eln component ReferenceEditQuantity shape Thanks for the quick reply Indeed changing the line from type CRCSample with apostrophe to type CRCSample solved the problem Thanks also for the hint about the deprecated ElnWithFormulaBaseSection I didn t see in NOMAD Metainfo Browser any deprecated flag but I ll change that Any advice for any alternative for the very convenient function quantity chemical formula as CompositeSystem does not do the same thing It s a bit hard for us to put in the deprecation warning but I think we need to improve there It s listed under legacy sections in the python file but I fully understand that most people won t check that You can have a look at the PureSubstance and PureSubstanceSection classes They should provide a lookup and normalization of a chemical formula A sample is very rarely a pure single material though so we typically recommend using CompositeSystem to describe samples and then use the PureSubstanceSection in the components list to list any material that makes up it You can see an example of this in the https nomad lab eu prod v1 gui analyze metainfo nomad REDACTED EMAIL which is a CompositeSystem It does becom a bit nested though https nomad lab

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text: use the PureSubstanceSection in the components list to list any material that makes up it You can see an example of this in the https nomad lab eu prod v1 gui analyze metainfo nomad REDACTED EMAIL which is a CompositeSystem It does becom a bit nested though https nomad lab eu prod v1 gui analyze metainfo nomad REDACTED EMAIL components REDACTED EMAIL pure substance molecular formula So if you want users to fill this field manually you could place a custom eln quantity on the top level of your schema and fill this nested quantity during the normalization We are working on simplifying this over nesting in version 2 of the base sections but unfortunately we have not yet finished the general normalization for those Thank you for the detailed answer Indeed the ELNSubstance or ELNSample has the feature of elemental composition I will also have a detailed look that again as I try them out before Just for clarification of the use case chemical formula breaks down any almost arbitrary crystal no chemical exact elemental composition e g Ra0 2F0 6B0 3 made up example in one rush and indexes the elements for the Explore Periodic table as really neat feature search capabilities for non chemist collaboration partners after some digging Yes the ELNSample pure substance PureSubstanceSection molecular formula does the exact same behavior https nomad lab eu prod v1 gui analyze metainfo nomad REDACTED EMAIL pure substance molecular formula Thanks again Topic closed

title: Preview HDF5 only works when logged in
text: Hello I recently published an upload containing DAMASK HDF5 files and minted a DOI on it https doi org REDACTED PHONE NOMAD REDACTED PHONE 1 When I am reaching one of the HDF5 files in the Files tab of one of its entries I can t preview it with the embedded H5 viewer and get an Authorization required 401 error message As the upload is public I would expect the file to be viewable by anyone but it doesn t seem to be the case However when I log myself in I can see the file without any problem Is this the expected behaviour Because to me as the data is public anyone should be able to see the content of the file whether the person is logged in or not ahmed ilyas tagging you in since you have been looking at the hdf5 preview in the new ui Any thoughts

title: Problem with SectionProxy
text: Hey I wanted to use a SectionProxy becasue I reference a Section before defining it in the same python script In the section UMR InternalSubstrate I have the following quantity standard substrate lot Quantity type SectionProxy UMR StandardSubstrateLot a eln dict component ReferenceEditQuantity And in the same script below I define the Section class UMR StandardSubstrateLot EntryData m def Section But the App Container is unhealthy and I get the following error nomad metainfo metainfo MetainfoError Type UMR schemas substrate UMR StandardSubstrateLot Section of nomad metainfo metainfo Quantity type Quantity is not a valid metainfo quantity type Any ideas what I am missing out here This are the full logs of the App Container Define UMR StandardSubstrateLot first But that s what I exactly don t want to can not do and why I want to use the SectionProxy Otherwise I could use a normal Reference If I understood it correctly the SectionProxy is used if you define a section with the section class itself For example if you have a section Process Step which can have Substeps with the same attributes you can do python class ProcessStep ArchiveSection m def Section label quantity name name Quantity type str a eln dict component StringEditQuantity steps SubSection section def SectionProxy ProcessStep repeats True

title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
text: Hi I get errors when processing data from raw files in a plugin after switching to develop branch 1 3 3 dev142 g56585ed05 Before on NOMAD v1 3 3 the data was processed without errors NOMAD develop seems to be less tolerant with incoming data e g does not convert 20 into a float raise ValueError f Cannot convert value to self dtype ValueError Cannot convert 20 to class numpy float64 I am now adjusting my file reader to explicitly convert the data to e g float for example above But still I was wondering if NOMAD should not convert 20 into a float 20 0 as it did before Markus Scheidgen Maybe a guess but could be worth a try add a dot cuz the string 20 otherwise is unclear and maps more to an integer than a float 20 is a different statement This is due to the new metainfo datatypes merged recently And yes they are less forgiving now Auto converting strings into numbers is really a stretch This conversion from string to float is really the parsers job Theodore Chang DM BITTE whats your opinion on this The type system internally tries to convert the input data into the target type In this particular case when a string 20 is given the type instance tries to check the following 20 str numpy float64 20 this results actual check to be something like 20 REDACTED PHONE due to the inexact nature of floats The new system tries to be as smart as possible but for cases like this the risk of loss of precision cannot be avoided I ll say the easy fix is assign the correct value to the quantity meaning that instead of 20 use numpy float64 20 Thanks for clarifying

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: Hi NOMAD Team maybe this feature request is a little bit orthogonal to the idea of NOMAD Oasis and data sharing By launching an Oasis to a dedicated user community there s maybe the need to protect the Search Entry navigation and also the API against unauthorized access This can be the case if one wants easy sharing of research data only within a community or facility without exploiting all public uploads to everyone who knows the IP of the Oasis AFAIK following files may be updated then hard coded in the oasis gui src components search SearchPage js import withLoginRequired from api export default SearchPage export default withLoginRequired SearchPage Please login to search all public available data nomad search py Restrict all entries API calls to authentication method with login credentials change the line in def owner es query in lines 608 if owner all if user id is None raise AuthenticationRequiredError Authentication required for owner value user q query published True q viewers query user id force groups True elif owner public if user id is None raise AuthenticationRequiredError Authentication required for owner value user q query published True query with embargo False elif owner visible if user id is None raise AuthenticationRequiredError Authentication required for owner value user q query published True query with embargo False q viewers query user id force groups True elif owner shared Maybe you can create a flag option to the configs nomad yaml like oasis is protected true to restrict the access on demand when deploying an oasis if one need this extended authorization protection What do you think Hi ron do So far we have not thought about this kind of usage Some people do limit access to the Oasis by only hosting it in an internal network which can be connected to only through e g a VPN credentials That seems like a very systematic way of limiting access In principle what you suggest is doable but we would need to investigate quite a lot of time to properly check and ensure that this flag correctly protects all of the endpoints etc So I would say that at this point we are not willing to implement this unless we hear from multiple sources that this would be a necessary feature I would encourage trying out other solutions for now restrict the access to the machine by requiring the use

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: the endpoints etc So I would say that at this point we are not willing to implement this unless we hear from multiple sources that this would be a necessary feature I would encourage trying out other solutions for now restrict the access to the machine by requiring the use of a VPN service OR then do not make any of the data publicly available but only share it with a limited set of users Is not the option oasis allowed users doing this With the setting I cannot access the oasis at all unless a user in this list is logged in Maybe I don t understand the question completely If I understood correctly the question was more about controlling access without a special whitelist that would need to be maintained But you are Laurenz Rettig definitely right about oasis allowed users with this list you can control accesss with a pretty fine granularity I did not realize we even had this Maybe ron do could comment if allowed users would be an option for you Implementing something like the proposed is protected would not actually be that hard if we simply follow a similar strategy to allowed users in the codebase Hi Lauri Hi Laurenz thank you for your reply and discussing my request Maybe I should explain in more detail that I am aiming for I m setting up an Oasis in a protected University network as one common data sharing platform for several groups hence a Collaborative Research Centre Therefore externals can only access the oasis with usage of an VPN tunnel Problem statement use case But also within the University network the public part of the oasis the gui search interface e g https nomad lab eu prod v1 oasis gui search eln and also the public API can be accessed by all people who know the IP address of the dedicated oasis and maybe they are not part of any associated working groups As there is a very nice convenient feature for Uploads in Step3 with the checkbox Edit visibility and access Enabling this will allow all users including guests without an account to view the upload even before it is published to share unpublished quite recent research data between the working groups for review not bothering you to select a special group or person Also enabling visibility of unpublished data allows for data

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: will allow all users including guests without an account to view the upload even before it is published to share unpublished quite recent research data between the working groups for review not bothering you to select a special group or person Also enabling visibility of unpublished data allows for data changes e g some data have been updated in contrast to published data in the oasis can not be altered step 5 So I am searching for a workflow to share all not yet publishable data to everybody who has access to the oasis but not exposing recent research data to the world university I also thought about using allowed users in the yaml file but there are more than 100 people to take care off and this can be cumbersome Also this does not solve the problem to restrict the search ability only to known users as the data will be still exposed Does it make sense to you How do you want to define the list of users that are allowed You somewhere have to provide this list Doing it with allowed users you have to restart the oasis when you want to change this list I believe And I do believe it also restricts the API to access these files but I am not 100 sure of this What would make sense is the option to use your own key cloak and restrict access to only logged in users Is that what you want I know the name of the scientists working for the Collaborative Research Centre that not a problem at all I also use a local keycloak instance to keep track of them and to provide the access and to delete if necessary In the future I will also bind the keycloak with our university identity management system to use a more official login access control If students or PI leave they will automatically loose the account and cannot access the oasis and vice versa if a new student arrives But anyway that s not the point the oasis has a public search functionality also the API giving you all information about meta data without an account This public sharing feature is nice and good for the official NOMAD instance but not adequate for data sharing between several groups in a protected environment I want to restrict the API call and gui search functionality only

title: Publishing DFT calculations from RSPt
text: Is it possible to publish data from DFT calculations done with RSPt code https www uu se en department physics and astronomy research materials theory code development It is not automatically recognised by NOMAD atm is there a work around to make it searchable in NOMAD we need to publish it in June ab initio Joseph Rudzinski maybe you can answer since Nathan doesn t seem to be available Hi Santa Pile thanks for your post Indeed there is no development yet for a RSPt parser however I will make a note of this code for our future development Can you give me a rough idea of the scope and user base of this code For your upcoming publication You can still upload your calculations to nomad now and utilize the base features overarching metadata like authors references etc You could even create a small eln entry to annotate your uploads with further metadata or descriptions In this way you would achieve a usage more similar to Zenodo but already with more transparency and integration into the community of similar data Then when a RSPt parser is developed we could even work together on this if your group uses this code a lot your uploads can still be reprocessed for full search functionality If you are interested in this route then I am happy to provide more guidance just let me know Hi Joseph Rudzinski thank you for your reply I ll go point by point Can you give me a rough idea of the scope and user base of this code Then when a RSPt parser is developed we could even work together on this if your group uses this code a lot your uploads can still be reprocessed for full search functionality We will have to ask our partner for whom we are doing the publishing We are doing it for a common EU project MaMMoS https github com mammos project for which we wanted to use NOMAD as a platform to openly store and publish our data Within this project we have all kinds of data including ab initio simulations and measurement data Our partner who is working with this code are from Uppsala university We can definitely work together on the parser and can organise an initial meeting to discuss that with them For your upcoming publication You can still upload your calculations to nomad now

title: Publishing DFT calculations from RSPt
text: ab initio simulations and measurement data Our partner who is working with this code are from Uppsala university We can definitely work together on the parser and can organise an initial meeting to discuss that with them For your upcoming publication You can still upload your calculations to nomad now and utilize the base features overarching metadata like authors references etc You could even create a small eln entry to annotate your uploads with further metadata or descriptions Thank you for the great advice though we are struggling exactly with that If by small eln you mean to use a custom yaml schema then we would very much appreciate if you could recommend us some base clusses that we could use maybe with DFT calculations to make the data at least somehow searchable So far we had some trouble figuring out how to find existing sections that we could use Because if we make everything custom it becomes difficult to find the data using NOMAD s search tools We asked Hampus already https discord com channels REDACTED PHONE REDACTED PHONE and he referred us here If you are interested in this route then I am happy to provide more guidance just let me know Yes Please 1 If your partner is willing to do a little bit of parser development for their code we would certainly be interested in a collaboration where we help get them going and if desired then work with them for a smooth transition to the central NOMAD repo 2 The answer to this depends a little if you are working on an Oasis or the central NOMAD and also what the desired search functionalities are understanding that they will certainly be limited Do you want to simply be able to query for RSPt calculations that were uploaded or something more than that 1 I think it is better to discuss that in a meeting It s a bit more complicated from our side 2 At the moment if we understood it correctly publishing from an OASIS is not possible because ours is not connected to DOI and transfer of data from OASIS to central NOMAD is not yet functioning please correct me if we got it wrong But that s what we ve clarified so far that s why we moved to trying putting our data directly on the central central NOMAD but in addition the central NOMAD must already have these metadata fields available somewhere just like the results section Just like one can upload data with a JSON file with data formatted under the NOMAD Metainfo language and a YAML schema the schema says NOMAD which metadata to create and the JSON which value to fill which field with So having a complete entry in an Oasis title: Pydantic validation errors in QueryEditQuantity
text: Recently when combining the filters for a query in QueryEditQuantity the processing leads to Pydantic validation errors Here s what happens In the ELN entry I use a quantity defined with the QueryEditQuantity annotations to build a query via the search dialog box I create a search using two filters let s say entry type JupyterAnalysis and upload id hash When I save the entry the quantity is processed successfully However if I save the entry again the processing breaks stating Pydantic validation errors Here s an example https nomad lab eu prod v1 oasis gui user uploads upload id fnyfkrg6ROa7DKX9zagsTw entry id cNGZ4fYfxbV19OiogeJQtRUqjGmS logs I have made an upload on the example oasis for this issue https nomad lab eu prod v1 oasis gui user uploads upload id fnyfkrg6ROa7DKX9zagsTw There are two entries one was saved only once first processing archive yaml while the other second processing archive yaml was saved again which led to breaking it Error is available in the logs of the broken entry In the provided upload one can see in the processed query of the second processing entry the and key is modified to op I think this is what fails the validation mainfile of the first processing entry main file of the second processing entry ahmed ilyas and I were trying to debug Maybe Lauri Himanen can help us identify the issue So the validation done here https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob develop nomad datamodel data py ref type heads L267 is failing Fixing this would be a matter of tracking down which part of the code is modifyig the query in the second processing run My guess would be that the model validata value model dump is creating this malformed version during the first processing I found the issue In the models for operators the name of the field is op while the actual name of the operator is defined as an alias for example here https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob develop nomad app v1 models models py ref type heads L118 When model dump is used it defaults to using op as the key Setting model dump by alias True fixes the issue Fixed here https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 2493

title: Python Code for API
text: Hello I want to write a code to extract all the compounds that have CO3 and OH I already had experience with API on Materials Project and I know how to extract the coordination enviorment of the compounds But I think here that the process is different So I just need help with the code specially the part that you define the request of the compounds having CO3 and OH Thanks for your help in advance Hi there I think NOMAD only supports search at the elemental level not at molecule group level You can get all the entries that have C O H elements with a query like json owner visible query results material elements exclusive C H O And then you might have to write a custom script to filter down this list based on whether CO3 and OH is present Maybe results materials chemical formula iupac property comes handy for this Here s a link to this property for one of the entries https nomad lab eu prod v1 gui search entries entry id xBgltO0hnlRlT3 AeoO7t2jmeV9p data results material chemical formula iupac

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
text: Is there currently a NOMAD way of saving a experimental measurement uncertainty assigned to a Quantity Eg mean value std If no I would imagine this will be useful for everyone handling experimental data Not yet but on the PhysicalProperty discussion we are talking about these statistics For now we are adding a second quantity next to it See for example https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad material processing blob main src nomad material processing general py L573 My recommendation is you can try it out now yourself either following Hampus Näsström and Area A implementation or doing it more abstract following Area C current def of PhysicalProperty https github com nomad coe nomad simulations blob develop src nomad simulations schema packages physical property py L69 Bear in mind 1 the proper implementation of PhysicalProperty might take some weeks months still 2 whatever you implement now it should be then refactor afterwards into this new structure I d say 2nd option is more appealing from my point of view as it will directly reflect on implementing something practical on the interoperability TF and it will allow you to be ready to refactor more consistently and easy for you First option might mean you need to change a lot of logic here and there I think Markus Scheidgen had some slides with a suggestion for statistics under a PhysicalProperty Thank you guys Fortunately it s not an urgent problem for me but it came up in more than one conversation so I was curious Note that experimental datasets in NOMAD parsed from NeXus are following the NeXus convention similar to what Hampus showed Errors or uncertainties on a data quantity DATA field are stored in the DATA errors field You can see it in the Ellipsometry example upload how it is represented in NeXus and also in NOMAD

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: Hi all based on my own recent experience both in using NOMAD for searching and visualizing data and in implementing new features see my other posts in issues and in forum comp and also based on discussions with colleagues we think it would be very desirable useful to have NOMAD periodically even not too often let s say once a year or so make an automatic re parsing of all its archives uploads or possibly only of those older than XXX some criterion to ensure that data archived on NOMAD have been parsed with reasonably up to date parsers As far as I understand this seems not to be the case currently so that on NOMAD there are some old archives which have been parsed at the time of their upload e g 10 years ago therefore with old parsers lacking several functionalities that were not present at that time and have been developed in the meantime and therefore reporting displaying plotting only a subset of the data that would be plotted by up to date parsers see also https discord com channels REDACTED PHONE REDACTED PHONE threads REDACTED PHONE is this correct This situation entails a lack of visibility of potentially available data and metadata when making queries a lack of internal coherence more or less complete sets of data shown depending on the age of the upload and is also rather confusing for users aiming at contributing with developments one is never sure of which of the functionalities present in a parser really work correctly until one makes a new upload of data from a given code Is this periodic re parsing of archives something you are considering It seems to me an issue of rather general interest and I can imagine several users have already asked for this so probably there are reasons for not having implemented it Or has it been implemented at least partially and perhaps I have just run into some pathological archives that had not been re parsed Thanks for any explanations Hi Elena Molteni thanks for reaching out about this I think something like 1 5 years ago we tried a relatively high scale reparsing effort This unfortunately it is not a fully automate process that we can trigger periodically and experience showed us that sometimes there are uploads that have entries that show some parsing errors This typically leads to further

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: something like 1 5 years ago we tried a relatively high scale reparsing effort This unfortunately it is not a fully automate process that we can trigger periodically and experience showed us that sometimes there are uploads that have entries that show some parsing errors This typically leads to further fixes in the parsers and triggering the reprocessing again I do agree though that this might be something to explore in the near future on how to automate these kind of pipelines I will make note of this for our future plans In the meantime are there certain uploads or datasets that you would find particularly important for you to be reprocessed

title: Recreating deleted upload on OASIS
text: Do we need to restore the mongodb if we want to reintroduce a deleted upload Or would it be enough to reintroduce the folder in the volumes and trigger a reprocessing from the CLI Depends MongoDB does contain information that cannot be recreated by simply reprocessing upload id entry id timestamps authors coauthors inclusion in datasets etc If you need to restore this you will need the MongoDB snapshot But if you don t care you can simply reprocess Hrmm okay I guess the main issue is if there are references to this upload and we then create a new one Is there a way to recreate and upload with a specified ID The way to recreate everythign with an existing ID would be to move the correctly named folder into volumes re add the data uploads entries into MongoDB from a backup and run nomad admin uploads index upload id to get ES populated with data about the entries

title: Regex in nomad.search
text: Is it possible to use regex queries in nomad search search I imagine it would be since the query ultimately gets passed to elasticsearch but im not able to find a query format that works Desired example query results eln lab ids pattern my regex search result search owner all query query user id archive metadata main author user id This seems to reduce to query results eln lab ids pattern my regex Any ideas Maybe Lauri Himanen has an idea Hi Rosjo No regular expressions are not allowed at the moment They are typically prohibitively slow when dealing with large amounts of data and our API does not allow their usage although it is technically possible Hi I have a follow up question on nomad search functionality Apparently after searching for some entries and getting some response if I look into those dictionaries I cannot find anywhere the quantities that are nested into subsections of my archive The entries I got in my response are originally generated from the ELN Note that the search API endpoint entries query will only return things stored in the search index the entries archive query will return the whole archive But I guess I would need to know more details okay thanks I ll check it better
