title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: If Entry is inheriting from ActivityStep its rendering in the Data Browser raises a TypeError of sectionDef is undefinied Lauri Himanen do you have any idea what could go wrong Could be again that they are not Schema Or is it already Schema or EntryData in an upper level Yes NeXus is a Schema What is put into data This Schema allows having NeXusMeasurements e g Mpes which are Measurements with an extra normalisation that sets the method used The NexusMeasurements have Entries which are ActivitySteps Note that I am normalising our Measurements so that NexusMeasurement steps inherited from Measurement steps hold the different ActivitySteps In this particular case there are 2 of them both are of type Mpes ENTRY which is subclass of Entry that is subclass of ActivityStep as shown on the first screenshot Without this normalisation they render properly and show the ActivityStep related features like step name Here there are no ActivityStep s registered below steps but as soon as I add a specialised one I got the error above Talking to Andrea we have found some working examples os steps e g as tey are used by Experiment BaseSection and its subclasses For https github com PDI Berlin pdi nomad plugin blob d68e1c408ec6c6ffdfdc6c7b5703f093e9cbac6b src pdi nomad plugin mbe processes py L1185 I have some notes Activity steps are defined as a Subsection of ActivityStep s https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob develop nomad datamodel metainfo basesections py ref type heads L352 Experiment steps inherited from Activity steps is redefined with the use of ExperimentStep as section def https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob develop nomad datamodel metainfo basesections py ref type heads L544 After an init metainfo call or during the assignment the corresponding python class may probably be fixed by adding and or overriding the extra Quantities as class properties activity and lab id Note that activity is a Quantity of type Activity and not of ActivityReference Also note that although ExperimentStep lab id is overriding BaseSection lab id so its eln label becomes different but a self lab id of an instance will still be assumed to be the same as BaseSection lab id e g by the BaseSection normalize method In the above code ExperimentMbePDI steps inherited from Experiment steps is overwritten by a Subsection of ActivityReference s This means that ANY inherited functionality

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: a self lab id of an instance will still be assumed to be the same as BaseSection lab id e g by the BaseSection normalize method In the above code ExperimentMbePDI steps inherited from Experiment steps is overwritten by a Subsection of ActivityReference s This means that ANY inherited functionality e g Experiment normalize which may use self steps will still assume that these are ActivityStep s or ExperimentStep s but certainly not that these are ActivityReference s I think these kind of overrides can cause lots of confusions in the python code and potential errors in functionality but might give hints to the GUI problem which is not implemented in python Note that if I use ActivityStep directly and not a subclass than the GUI seems to work Note the difference on how the GUI receives the data I m sorry Sandor Brockhauser but I can t really follow your problem We are specializing the steps subsection with specialized ActivityStep section throughout all our schemas I don t really follow what the issue is Maybe you have to explain it to me over a call I was able to reproduce the problem locally using some MPES data as an example My initial guess is that this is related to the fact that pynxtools nomad schema Mpes ENTRY is not a regular section definition but seems to be what is called an inner section definition ENTRY is defined within the Mpes class Is this the case Sandor Brockhauser I can try and see if there is a way to fix this What was the original reason for introducing inner section definitions in NeXus and does that still apply If it is possible to implement the NeXus schema without inner sections definitions we should do it Hampus Näsström Does Area A use inner section definitions This feature is not even documented at the moment really there is only one example in examples metainfo advanced concepts py No as far as I know we don t use any inner section definitions Sandor Brockhauser There is now a MR where I introduced a fix https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 2259 Could you test it out Before we merge that we will need to also test that MR with some other complicated archives to make sure that I did not break anything in other schemas Thank you for

title: Reproducibility indicators bug from afm nexus file
text: in an application definition might not be best practice although also used similarly e g in NXspe or NXem This could produce similar problems Lukas Pielsticker Markus Kuehbach Ok I didn t know this possible issue indeed I followed the data schema present in the definitions of the pynxtools It is indeed a bug either in our handling of NXcollection or in the application definition which we need to discuss I think This should not actually be a problem of the parsing of nxs files in NOMAD we don t have any special rules in place for NXcollection it is is only considered special in the validation I also see that now read nexus gives DEBUG FIELD entry reproducibility indicators cantilever oscillator oscillator excitation HDF5 dataset oscillator excitation shape type i8 DEBUG value 10 DEBUG classpath NXentry NXcollection NXcomponent DEBUG NOT IN SCHEMA DEBUG DEBUG ATTRS entry reproducibility indicators cantilever oscillator oscillator excitation units DEBUG value V DEBUG classpath NXentry NXcollection NXcomponent DEBUG NOT IN SCHEMA DEBUG DEBUG GROUP entry reproducibility indicators cantilever oscillator phase lock loop NXafm NXentry NXcollection NXcomponent NXlockin HDF5 group entry reproducibility indicators cantilever oscillator phase lock loop 4 members DEBUG classpath NXentry NXcollection NXcomponent DEBUG NOT IN SCHEMA etc Which is also true as there is only the NXcollection defined there So anything below is indeed not documented and will not be parsed If you want to make it available in Nomad you would need to define possible reproducibility indicators either by adding them to the appdef or by defining a base class for it The problem is related to the linking Essentially in NXafm ENTRY reproducibility indicators we only define cantilever oscillator but none of its sub items NOMAD will then only try to parse the cantilever oscillator group from the link but not anything that is inside that Hdf5 group This is the general case for links if you want to have all sub elements also show up at the link destination you need to explicitly define them in application definition as well If you run our HDF5 validator you also get warnings like Field entry reproducibility indicators cantilever oscillator phase lock loop cantilever phase positioner has no documentation meaning that this concept is not defined NOMAD does not parse any elements that are title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: Hey if a nomad quantity is of dtype float it might contain nan values These are stored as null in the archive json Then parsing the archive leads to an error see attached This errors is caused by trying to cast an numpy object array containing None values to the numpy float dtype because of safe casting Can you have a look I think Theodore Chang DM BITTE would know most about this I guess we should allow null values to be read in as NaN I guess JSON does not have NaN primitive i talked to theodore already in python json works well with nan how do you create the json which you write on disc Lauri Himanen theodore says if json doesnt store it doesnt work that would be quite an issue What do you mean with in Python JSON works well with NaN Officially the JSON specs don t contain anything for NaN https www json org json en html I think the problem might be solvable by having an implicit rule that any null value for a numeric quantity gets turned into NaN when deserializing But this is where we need Theodore s opinion i emant that this works python a ab np array 1 2 3 np nan tolist r np array json loads json dumps a ab 1 type r outputs numpy float64 and json dumps outputs ab 1 0 2 0 3 0 NaN so in python json store it as NaN but maybe that is not comliant with the general json specs ah yes it says here json dumps has an allow nan parameter which defaults to True I see Since we use the JSON archives also in Javascript I think we have to use fully JSON spec compliant output ah i see then i will go back to theodore who was not so open to relaxing the strict type checking since also inf is not permitted i am fearing that when updating to a newer nomad version with this strict typsetting will create plenty of issues right now i am still running on a version from before that if then all null values will break we can not update Come on Lauri Himanen and Theodore Chang DM BITTE we can t have it like this We should just relax the type checking so that null becomes nan This becomes a much

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: running on a version from before that if then all null values will break we can not update Come on Lauri Himanen and Theodore Chang DM BITTE we can t have it like this We should just relax the type checking so that null becomes nan This becomes a much larger issue when the front end is involved in the picture Could you Micha create an issue about this in Gitlab and we continue the discussion there I think the only clean solution would be to maybe have some flavor of JSON that supports NaN and Infs but this might not be so easy Just converting NaN and Inf to null will not do as that leads to information loss i can create an issue and tag it here https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2128 If null is nan what about inf What have we done until you introduced the breaking changes We can t have the processing of old archives break on existing oases The we need to put some sort of check for backwards compatibility and treat that as we have doen until now The ideal solution would be if non editable quantites are never written to disc but always loaded or setby the normalizer it doesnt make sense anyhow to store a non editable quantity whihc is derived from raw data into json then on load it is always np nan and np inf Ah i have been facing this issue and scratching my head all along I am glad that it s a known issue The error leads to the entire data section not being found making it hard to find the problem We need slightly better error handling here I don t think that we have a solution here I am not even sure if there is concensus on the issue and the scope of the problem I tried to categorize the problem space a bit Please comment on the issue https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2128 note 282437

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: The old gui does not check what has been modified in NOMAD ELN by user rather suggests pushing the whole entry to be saved in the mainfile This case problem because lots of information should not be part of the main file but rather derived during its parsing and normalisation As a result functions those create such derived information shall be very careful See the example in the Activity base section if self method archive results eln methods append self method else archive results eln methods append self m def name if archive workflow2 is None archive workflow2 Workflow name self name archive workflow2 tasks step to task for step in self steps Note that results eln methods list is appended but workflow2 tasks list is overwritten results seem to be the place where normalisation is expected to output so it is not saved to the mainfile On the other hand workflow2 is expected to be provided directly so it is saved to and then fetched from the mainfile The problem is that Activity and other Base Sections do not follow the rule but instead their normalisation functions output to workflow2 How do they handle the situation that some other sources may have dropped here something what these base sections were not prepared to They simply remove everything from here independent if it was a useful thing or not and populate the workflow2 with their own stuff In this way even 2 base section cannot coexists in an Archive since one will remove the output of the another one but it cannot coexists with anything else which would like to serve information to workflow2 This results that every base section must stay alone in its own private Entry What should have happened instead A Workflow Card could handle workflows from the results section So any normalisation which would like to produce a workflow for being displayed it could drop its derived workflow to results where its expected place is Which is by the way not effected by the GUI bug Changing base sections to normalise to archive results workflow2 is a quick fix Making a Workflow Card which picks up the data from archive results workflow2 and not or not only from archive workflow2 is also quick and easy Any counterarguments Andrea Albino Markus Scheidgen Lauri Himanen Hi yes this problem needs indeed to be solved We could address this

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: a quick fix Making a Workflow Card which picks up the data from archive results workflow2 and not or not only from archive workflow2 is also quick and easy Any counterarguments Andrea Albino Markus Scheidgen Lauri Himanen Hi yes this problem needs indeed to be solved We could address this in the MR https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2053 More thoughts on that are that we were discussing about a Normalizer plugin that is in charge of filling Results section and so one can think to a Workflow normalizer too I think we discussed this option some time ago when discussing with Lauri and Hampus about the possible weakness of a normalize method that simoultaneously touches the Data Results and Workflow Having separate normalizer creates a separation in the steps of the various archive section filling in I agree with Andrea Let s deal with the overwriting when we tackle the refactoring of the basesection normalize methods Let me mark this base section problem as a bug because this forbids the generation of the workflow in our experiment entries While thinking about a complete redesign and implementing it we also need a quick fix which demonstrates the the proper workflow generation as something provided by and inherited from the basesections Alternatively we could just implement the correct behaviour in new classes which DO NOT inherit normalisation from base sections but the whole point in using base sections is that the functionalities are inherited Changed the channel name Saving bug in old GUI normalisation to workflow2 Note title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: Lauri Himanen When I try to focus on a specific region in a scatterplot in the catalysis app the filter does not get updated in the same way as I can see it does in the production of the Solar Cells app Both regarding the update of the range in the window but also regarding the displayed entry list I think this is related to the repeating subsections and array quantities but I am not sure if this is the intended behaviour or a bug Can I tests this in the Oasis Which scatter plot Note that when you select a region in the scatter plot you are essentially setting a filter like 500 K temperature 600 K AND 50 conversion 80 If temperature is an array this will include entries where temperature is e g 300 400 500 But maybe first let me know how to reproduce this and then we can see if there is indeed a bug or if the behaviour is simply unexpected and should be reconsidered Yes you can test this in the Test Oasis https nomad lab eu prod v1 oasis gui search heterogeneouscatalyst results properties catalytic reaction reaction conditions temperature 5Bgte 5D REDACTED PHONE 20K results properties catalytic reaction reaction conditions temperature 5Blte 5D REDACTED PHONE 20K it does show the first filter e g for the temperature but not the other axis e g the feed composition it also does not update the range that it shows in the widget partly it is because an entry that falls within the search window also has data points outside but there is some problem for the 2nd axis probably because it is not specified further e g reactants conversion so it looks like it does not attempt any filtering along the 2nd axis Yes looks like somehow the filter for one of the axis is not set correctly that is definitely a bug Any idea what we should do in the case where we filter for a value in an array Maybe we keep all the entries that contain at least one match in the array but we should simply zoom in on the region and not show the rest of the points that did not match Or do you think we should require all of the points in that array to match the criterion Yes the first would be the desired effect Keep

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: the array but we should simply zoom in on the region and not show the rest of the points that did not match Or do you think we should require all of the points in that array to match the criterion Yes the first would be the desired effect Keep all entries that have at least one point in the desired region But zoom in Thanks I was able to fix the bug but I think I cannot create a very good solution for the other problem I think we have to separate the current Focus on region into two different actions Zoom and Filter Zoom will simply zoom in on the selected region without affecting any of the filters Filter would create filters from the current selection and if autorange true the plotted region changes accordingly The reason why the Filter results may seem weird is the fact that filters simply return all entries where there is some data that fullfills the query It does not care about where in the data the condition is filled and will always just return the full entry This is why if you create a filter based on results properties catalytic reaction reaction conditions temperature results properties catalytic reaction reactants gas concentration in the plot may also contain other reactions simply because they are included in this entry where one of the reactions matched the query If we want we could try to anticipate these unexpected results by disabling the Filter action completely when dealing with non scalar data You would still be able to zoom in on the data I think I understand Do you think it would be possible to update the legend marker to just include the labels for those entries with points in the focused region Filter or Zoom If I want to use results properties catalytic catalyst catalyst name as the marker That would be very helpful Let me see what I can do

title: Search in data section
text: Th changes are actually minimal but I had to spend quite some time to understand the structure and how definitions are built during import from the python classes so we can do the exact opposite first the definitions and than the classes just like in case of creating the schema from yaml The actual code which indeed required this change is here in reload quantities dynamic elasticsearch extension py line 530 if isinstance section Section and issubclass section section cls EntryData This code only takes sections of a package and their schema tree if the Section is also an EntryData Note that this function is called 15 times during an appworker startup and each times it walks through the whole tree of all these sections Without NeXus we have only 815 search quantities here so it might not be a big deal but when we want to search for thousands of quantities it is probably worth optimising it a bit Also note that in this code we simply check quantities independently and create corresponding search quantities Next to avoid unnecessary traverses this is also an ideal case for parallelisation Currently a single core is used and this results in a long startup if lots of search qauntities are to be registered This I can try to help Anyway unfortunately registering all these has not solved the issue After parsing a nexus file the data appears in metadata search quantities and Himanen Is it expected that the elemental composition from results material cannot be used as a marker in a scatterplot I am trying to get a plot with the mass fraction of a certain element as marker color in a scatterplot but it doesn t seem to work I can use e g results material n elements or use a label e g results material elements 1 but if I try to use results material elemental composition 0 element I get as marker undefined and if I change from element to mass fraction I get an empty plot Although those properties are filled for those entries in the results section I was able to use results material elemental composition 0 element as a marker color I will send some screenshots Your issue could be caused by some of the following reasons Scatter plots only sample up to a certain size whatever you have defined in the scatter plot setup and if none of these samples have this data set you will only see undefined Maybe you have some additional filters that prevent the data from entering the scatter plot Maybe your choice of x y axes is contradictory with the marker color maybe results material elemental composition 0 element is not set whenever your x y value is set Thanks a title: Suggestions in Inputfields
text: Hey can someone tell me when the Terms WIdgets with Input fields allows suggestions based on already type information Hi Micha I believe this is only possible for those terms defined in the results py file in the central software which have an added property a elasticsearch Elasticsearch suggestion default as e g here name Quantity type str shape description Name of the product preferably the IUPAC name a elasticsearch Elasticsearch material entry type Elasticsearch suggestion default I could be wrong and there might be another way for terms from plugin definitions That would explain it thanks is there a possibility to enable this for custom quantities Julia is correct We only collect suggestions for data stored in results Because the custom quantities use a different storage mechanism which allows better scaling to multiple schemas base class searches adding suggestions to them does not work in the same way There is a technical solutions that would make this possible but it will be much slower We are not prepared to explore this much further at this point in time Ok I understand

title: Switch view between Data and Plot
text: Hi If you are using PlotSection as documented here https nomad lab eu prod v1 staging docs reference annotations html plot you should have a figures subsection in your data that contains the full plot data as JSON You can see an example here https nomad lab eu prod v1 staging gui search solarcells entry id t3X o QsCZ7EzUsA3gnvTOmQJTX data data figures 0 figure You can of also store the raw data for your plots in a separate quantity in your schema if you prefer having it in a more structured way It is quite typical that your Plots are actually generated from a combination of several quantities

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: I was wondering what the expierence with nomad updates is When i am updating plugins reloads are not helping but we have to do strg shift R Is it possible to force a proper reload of nomad also when plugins have changes is someone else experiencing that There are two potential causes for things not updating 1 The actual GUI code is only updated when the GUI has changed There is a hash based on the GUI used as the name for the Javascript file that the browser is loading Those are basically cached indefinetely But this should not be a problem has the GUI code has not changed 2 The GUI uses a resource called artifacts js and env js Those are dynamically generated by the nomad app at start up time The artifacts js contains all the schemas and stuff I assume this is what actually gives you trouble There is caching on these because they can be quite large But the caching is supposed to be based on an etag which again is based on a hash of the artifacts js content This etag should change when you plugins change Maybe something is wrong here Your browsers network tab you can see if a resource is loaded from cache or not On the first reload after a change you should see that the artifacts js has actually been loaded from the server In your network tab you should see these response headers on the artifacts js Cache Control max age 60 must revalidate Etag W 969f39d2e790f15232c0bbe87463fc6c The must revalidate tells the browser if the last cached result is older than 60 seconds it should go to the server If this happens the Etag is compared If the Etag has not changed the backend will only respond with 304 Not Modified If the Etag has changed what should happen if you modified a plugin schema for example it should return 200 including the actual content I did a small update of the schema hiding a quantity it did not update until i clicked strg ctrl R the etag didnt change Your screenshoots don t say if it was a cached result or a real one Did you had a look You have to use this view Here you can see if it is memory cache or not it also shows you the return code e g 304 If

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: didnt change Your screenshoots don t say if it was a cached result or a real one Did you had a look You have to use this view Here you can see if it is memory cache or not it also shows you the return code e g 304 If you open the artifacts js alone e g new tab with https nomad lab eu prod v1 gui artifacts js and if you wait for 60 seconds before sending the network tab should always look like this Either it is 304 with a tiny size etag did not change or 200 with a large size etag changed If it says memory cache in the size column than you have not waited 60 s Ok I have to test again then The Etag was the same and only after refresh with shift it was displayed correctly But I didn t wait 60s Would it then reload even without a simple reload Strg r I dont get it In the update i hide lab id Only the last reload is with strg shift r and then it is displayed correctly but the ressources still says from cache somethinge doesnt add up You really have to look at artifacts js isolated in a separate tab If you load the whole NOMAD GUI it will load everything twice Its so fast you probably don t notice With this you will always see Aus cache But this might just mean the version you just loaded a few milliseconds before Anyhow the etag should really change when you change a python schema update 60s So if you are seeing the same etag there is a problem Does the etag change after your hard reload shift ctr r What schema are you changing exactly Maybe it is for some reason not part of the etag even though everything known to the gui should be in there The etag doenst change even after hard reload I am changing a plugin schema the one you are seeing on screen i hide the lab id it is visible until i do a hard reload Looking at the code its pretty obvious Sorry that i put you through this and did not realize this earlier I am pretty sure now that this is a bug I open an issue and fix it I am just confused that this was not raising

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: do a hard reload Looking at the code its pretty obvious Sorry that i put you through this and did not realize this earlier I am pretty sure now that this is a bug I open an issue and fix it I am just confused that this was not raising problems earlier For the current impl I only based the etag on the config My thinking that this is enough because the nomad lab version and git commit is part of that But in the light of plugins and not everything being a submodules anymore the artifacts js can easily change via a plugin change not reflected in the config This MR is using the artifact js directly to compute the etag That should fix the issue https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 1714 Hey markus thank you this was raising issues and we communicated it but not directly with you apparently D I think this is one of the issues the right person needs to see i told people to use strg shift r i checked with hampus we thought that ctrl shift r was the solution Ctrl shift r is always a solution But seriously I hope the fix above will solve this issue and people can work with your oasis without ctrl shift r yes a simple reload now does the job thanks

text: lab relies on MDanalysis anymore but plugins like simulation workflow schema which is in the central deployments do depend on this Do we still need the dependency in requirements plugins txt or it s enough that the plugin depends on it within it s toml 2 FAIRmat NFDI nomad distro dev is only used to configure your local development environments so it has no effect on any deployments For the versions used in deployments it only comes from this pyproject toml https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad distro blob main pyproject toml ref type heads file and the requirements https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad distro blob main requirements txt ref type heads file generated based on the same toml file So no other repositories are involved there ahmed ilyas Thank you for the explanation and sorry for not acknowledging sooner I think I am starting to understand this better at least in principle What I don t understand still is the following In the toml that you linked it says nomad parser plugins atomistic 1 0 But the requirements file says nomad parser plugins atomistic 1 0 2 via nomad distribution pyproject toml It looks like these were updated a week ago But the atomistic parsers has several versions above this e g v1 0 3 from 3 weeks ago Why is this not updating in the requirements and how can I fix this The toml gives a valid set of distributions the requirements txt is generated using the scripts generate python dependencies sh script But if you want a specific version of nomad parser plugins atomistic in the requirements file you can bump up the version in the toml file and generate the requirements txt file I don t want a specific version though I just always want the latest release version I thought this would already happen with nomad parser plugins atomistic 1 0 in the toml The question is why is this stuck at the 1 0 2 release In the scripts generate python dependencies sh it will only upgrade nomad lab and nomad lab gui no plugins are upgraded automatically so they will always keep their locked version The intention here was that no other dependency gets automatically updated and individual plugin authors can upgrade their plugins as they see fit and we have a more controlled roll out Otherwise if

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: and nomad lab gui no plugins are upgraded automatically so they will always keep their locked version The intention here was that no other dependency gets automatically updated and individual plugin authors can upgrade their plugins as they see fit and we have a more controlled roll out Otherwise if random plugins get updated automatically during the nightly run the pipeline might start failing due to changes in the plugin Solved Thanks ahmed ilyas To summarize my confusion and the solution As Ahmed explains above the distro dev toml controls the versions on the central deployments The somewhat unclear aspect for me is that the used in the toml do not imply that a plugin version will be automatically updated upon a new release In contrast the versions will be locked once the requirements file is created The is used for flexible versioning between interdependent plugins To change the version one must increase the version x x x and then recreate the requirements file with the update python dependencies script followed by a MR

title: Unwanted warning for NumberEditQuantity of unit='dimensionless'
text: I have a NumberEditQuantity defined as follows py common beam mask Quantity type int description common beam mask a eln component NumberEditQuantity minValue 0 maxValue 100 unit dimensionless In the GUI when I click and select the Unit div and then un select by clicking elsewhere a warning in red pops up Unit is required attaching a screencast The warning is not desirable in this case But dimensionless is a unit What happens when you click the downarrow there s nothing in the dropdown I think we can remove the unit div altogether in case unit dimensionless just like we have it when no unit is specified or fill the unit div with dimensionless yeah or add some symbol still the quantity has units so for some stuff you might wanna do common bean mask magnitude yeah exactly Does is work if you simply ommit the unit in the schema It works fine in that case however the quantity is not of type pint Quantity in that case I would just remove it for now That s what we have everywhere else I think dimensionless still implies some sort of quantity And here you just have an ID or what is this But definitely something that should be fixed long term Maybe write an issue in Gitlab A better quantity description might also be needed as you can tell Or is it the fraction of the beam that is blocked Yeah if it was a mere ID having an int type makes total sense But this is a value coming from the instrument and ideally should be treated as a quantity for consistency with other quantities I am not 100 percent sure but i think its a fraction I think we should probably have a unit for attenuation there are other quantities like gain values which have the same issue Okay just write an issue on Gitlab for the gui problem I guess and the unit itself we can discuss next week when I m back alright

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD

message detail No file name provided and the file does not look like a zip or tar file Which doesn t appear if I compress my nexus file into a zip file for example However from what I checked the requests module seems to be able to take all kind of file or maybe by AI helper lied which happens Do you know if there s an option I should modify in the call to requests the code I executed import Nomad API token Nomad API get authentication token https nomad lab eu prod v1 api v1 REDACTED EMAIL MyPassword Nomad id Nomad API upload to NOMAD http REDACTED PHONE nomad oasis api v1 token test interface D18 nxs And the upload to NOMAD function looks like that def upload to NOMAD nomad url token upload file Upload a single file for NOMAD upload e g zip format with open upload file rb as f try response requests post nomad url uploads headers Authorization f Bearer token Accept application json data f timeout 30 upload id response json get upload id if upload id return upload id print response is missing upload id print response json return except Exception print something went wrong uploading to NOMAD return Hi Julien The uploads POST endpoint docs says the following There are two basic ways to upload files in the multipart formdata or streaming the file data in the http body Both are supported Note however that the second method only allows the upload of a single file and that it does not transfer a filename If a transfer is made using method 2 you can specify the query argument file name to name it This needs to be specified when using method 2 unless you are uploading a zip tar file for zip tar files the names don t matter since they are extracted So I think your problem might be resolved by

title: Uploads are not displayed, despite being in the volumes folder
text: I have the problem that my test uploads are not displayed anymore in my local test oasis despite being present in the corresponding volumes folder on the disk This happened after I started another test oasis in a different directory For this purpose I deleted all the docker volumes but not the files so that the old uploads are not displayed in my new test oasis anymore Now I wanted to check the old test oasis again and thought that I would still be able to see the old uploads there because they are still in the volumes folder But they are not displayed Is this a logic error from my side or does someone know how to fix this Thanks Hi Aaron Schüller Ruhl The docker volumes contain all the information that we store in databases and in our search index Without this information the raw data and the archives stored in the volumes folder data will not be available It is still possible to somewhat recover your data by using the raw data If you don t have thousands of uploads the simplest method is to move the fs folder out of the volumes folder and then re upload them individually They will then get reprocessed and assigned new identifiers Note that the staging folder contains unpublished uploads and the public folder contains published ones There are also programmatic ways to achieve something similar so if you wish to recover a lot of data ask me for further instructions In the future if you wish to make a backup of your data so that you are easily able to fully recover it follow the guide here https nomad lab eu prod v1 staging docs howto oasis admin html backups Thanks Lauri Himanen then this was a misunderstanding from my side Is there a chance to keep the upload id because of references Restoring data with the same upload id or other ids like entry id is quite tricky Theodore Chang DM BITTE Is it possible to provide fixed ids using the bundle format Can upload bundles be generated easily just from the data stored in volumes The final brute force alternative is to modify the values directly in MongoDB and in the produced archives It is possible but needs quite a bit of work Bundles also pull data from mongo solely from volumes is not possible Upload id

title: Uploads stuck in processing phase
text: I have created a plugin with a specific parser for my data you can find it here https github com leonardomusini nomad plugin mbe git I have installed the local nomad oasis with the nomad distro dev repository Until now everything worked fine and I could upload my files and visualize them with success But now I m experiencing a problem in the upload of files they remain stuck in processing phase with statuses RUNNING or PENDING I have tried to delete them in the Uploads page but this error appear Unexpected error The upload is still being processed Please try again and let us know if this error keeps happening I need help to solve this issue Also this other similar error appears The upload is still being processed 400 Hi Leonardo If you have updated your nomad distro version there might be a discrepancy somewhere You need to check the logs in your app container to get a better view do you see any error If they are stuck in your local oasis you can reset them using nomad admin reset processing or nomad admin uploads reset upload id There are other options as well please check documentation on https nomad lab eu prod v1 docs reference cli html nomad admin

title: Uploads to NOMAD never finish processing
text: I used GROMACS version 2024 2 I could of course try to reupload the files in a new Upload I agree this happened to me on Friday too Lauri Himanen do you know of any solution here when the upload gets stuck in processing We can forcefully reset the processing stage there are CLI commands for doing this Unfortunately the regular users do not have any mechanism for doing this but it needs to be the distro admin What kind of data do you Luis Walter have in those two stuck uploads Would you like me to try and reprocess them now If they keep on consistently failing it could be that the parser is running into trouble But we will see The data are GROMACS biomembrane simulations for a potential of mean force calculation Yes it would be great if you could try to just reprocess them now ah right well in any case I believe the situation is as follows 1 When your upload hangs it is likely just an anomali in processing and often times simply needs to be reprocessed In this case you need to request us to do this for you here 2 The error arising in your processing has to do with using Gromacs2024 Unfortunately we cannot fully support Gromacs2024 until MDAnalysis supports it since a significant portion of the Gromacs processing relies on MDAnalysis We would be able to parse some info e g the input parameters and energy file but the topology storage and visualization will not be possible I think at the moment the whole system visualization will be disabled Bernadette Mohr is going to put in a fix which makes the Gromacs2024 a bit more clear and avoid some of this error causing processing so that we somewhat ease the situation However if you want the full functionality for these uploads later on you will have to have them reprocessed i e you should probably keep track of which uploads you are making that are 2024 for reprocessing in the future Luis Walter Unfortunately we are also experiencing connection issues with the MPCDF servers But as soon as the connections have been fixed I will issue the reprocessing Remind me here if you don t hear from me I just had a meeting with someone from MPI MPCDF seems to be completely down atm Joseph Rudzinski Alright Could you comment

section repeats the capitalization disappears Markus Scheidgen Joseph Rudzinski Yes this is a new feature and should be consistent with quantities in the ELN which previously already got capitalized when you look into the metainfo tab at the end you want to resolve some path run calculation band structure electronic which with these changes is not clear at all However the capitalization and the style of the ELN is not perfect I will not take it as a reference to change what was there before ELN serves one purpose which is prepare and upload data for one part of the users the data info should serve the purpose of finding the data that you already stored that s why the ELNs when pressing the Save button will follow the archive data style of non capitalized underscores What I mean with this is that in the ELN everything is capitalized and clear makes sense for the data tab I am not 100 sure of it I agree what you say The only problem is that the ELN is in the data tab data section so it might not be easy to distinguish the 2 uses so here in this case it would look better to have rendering for the subsection names I disagree What happens when you press save there btw with Data tab I meant the metainfo trees we shared not the section At the end this is something that data experts will use and for them might be easier to see the pythonic underscore and non capital names ELNs is just another use which serve another purpose mohammad nakhaee is being fixed with https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 1693 It is not intentional The sub sections should show the name of the sub section And those names are lower case It is now either capitalizing the sub section name or more likely it is using the section name e g the name of the sub

title: Weird GUI data info
text: FAIR merge requests 1693 It is not intentional The sub sections should show the name of the sub section And those names are lower case It is now either capitalizing the sub section name or more likely it is using the section name e g the name of the sub section type instead of the sub sectoin name I ll say that is confusing it forces us to keep the same m def name for sub section If I have k mesh SubSection Mesh and r mesh SubSection Mesh are both Mesh Also from a data science point of view what I want to see is k mesh or r mesh not K Mesh nor R Mesh Again ELN serves the purpose of human filling blanks The archived data is a different thing imho I think the problem is now that all subsections also in Run and Metadata are rendered with capital letters but for the quantity it is only applying capitalization and removal in the Data section Removing the might be ok but the general capitilization of all sub sections and quantities needs to be reverted As ChemaP says if you are somewhat familiar with the underlying schema this rename becomes really really confusing Section names and sub section names are sometimes very similar often it is just the capitalization that helps to separate them Therefore to keep them mentally separated the capital or not capital is important We can find solutions to customize specific ELNs but we cannot apply this to the whole metainfo archive I actually also think the capitalization is not so important for the ELN but removing the underscores in the ELN will make a big difference for lab scientists I do think quantity names should have the same rules as subsection names I agree on the consistency We should then opt to leave everything un capitalized This will bring them closer to how they are defined in the schema As mentioned many times before we need the option to add annotations like label to the SubSection constructor I think this would solve all this At the end NOMAD is a Python schema so I would go pythonic all these are attributes of a class called EntryArchive hence it should follow the typical convention uncapitalized with underscores as separators Hampus Näsström But the label quantity would be meant for edge cases not for breaking reasonable

title: Weird GUI data info
text: At the end NOMAD is a Python schema so I would go pythonic all these are attributes of a class called EntryArchive hence it should follow the typical convention uncapitalized with underscores as separators Hampus Näsström But the label quantity would be meant for edge cases not for breaking reasonable conventions I guess there is tension between staying true to the technicalities of the schema and providing human readable labels But right now the middle ground is so blurry that it is more confusing than helpful Yes but I am fine with keeping the quantity convention as long as it can be overwritten by a label annotation So we are reverting the default back to the actual schema name and give you something to overwrite it with a human readable label Don t we have this already Or just for quantities Just for quantities We don t allow any annotations in the SubSection constructore as far as I can tell And this is consistently used in editable sections i e ELNs and in read only sections Not sure I follow Until now the quantity py some quantity Quantity a eln ELNAnnotation component ELNComponentEnum has been shown in the gui as Some quantity While py some quantity Quantity has been shown in the gui as some quantity As we don t have any annotations possible for py some sub section SubSection it has always been displayed as some sub section Ok apparently the ELN and the non ELN are not consistent ELNs humanize the shown names and the normal archive browser keeps the original technical names Exactly Assuming that we actually want this The ELN quantity and the archive quanity are two implementations The sub section has only one So the recent changes to the sub section fixed it for ELNs but broke it for the archive viewer And this is where ChemaP opened this post I m not sure what we want to be honest I would like to be able to give annotations like default display unit and label also to non ediatable quantities like arrays which we typically don t make editable in an ELN schema And also to sub sections so that I can for example set the label or include a sub section into the overview page The wish for one mechanism to apply a custom label and then having this mechanism applied consistently to both

title: Weird GUI data info
text: don t make editable in an ELN schema And also to sub sections so that I can for example set the label or include a sub section into the overview page The wish for one mechanism to apply a custom label and then having this mechanism applied consistently to both the ELN and the archive seem reasonable However I see two problems 1 It opens the door for you to have one schema using completely different conventions then the next But I guess this is the downside of any customization I can live with it 2 You will always find people that do not want to see the humanized labels but the raw technical keys Which means we probably need an option for the user to switch between labels and raw keys or somehow show them both I think somehow showing them both would be nice but all this might be too much patching for the old GUI I personally would be fine with having the raw keys as default for both quantities and sub sections as long as I can always specify a label in the schema if I want to But that is just my opinion and I m pretty sure others would disagree I agree with Hampus Näsström we need to be able to put a label for the sub sections Ok when there is the time I will revert the last change and instead add a possibility to have a custom label on both the quantities and subsections I guess this should also satisfy ChemaP That sounds good Thanks I had at the brief look at this in the code This will be a more complex thing For now I revert the changes that started this and we will have a more detailed discussion and solution once mohammad nakhaee is back Issue https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 1915 Revert MR https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 1703

title: When using the m_copy method, there are problems with the attributes overwriting
text: me and Hampus Näsström were getting these sort of errors Error Units do not match mol minute meter 3 second when defining such a quantity class VolumetricFlowRate TimeSeries value TimeSeries value m copy value unit meter 3 second set value TimeSeries set value m copy set value unit meter 3 second if we redefine the quantities this error does not arise Thing is that the unit mol minute is not in a parent class but in several sibling ones This bug report is just to point out the odd behaviour of m copy We can keep redefining quantities in the meanwhile that this issue is sorted out Could you create a minimal example with which we can reproduce the problem You can then post this as a GitLab issue directly

title: Where to find available base sections for yaml
text: https discord com channels REDACTED PHONE REDACTED PHONE another stupid question if we find smth that we could use there where do we find how to use this class in the base sections in a yaml file like for Chemical in Yaml we put nomad datamodel metainfo eln Chemical there is no parser for it in central NOMAD and it s not possible to publish in OASIS yet so we are trying to get it into a central NOMAD and make it searchable to publish it we need to publish it in June That name is a bit hard to find at the moment but we re working on making it more accessible For now you can see it when you hover over that class name but you can t copy paste that For something to copy paste you can either look at the url which will contain the path or click the button and search for the qualified name But again for DFT I really wouldn t try to define an ELN but instead work on a parser You will still need the parser But again I would write in the computation forum and they will be able to help you get started

title: Writing tests for plugin and parsing
text: in our plugins the parsers look for samples bei results eln lab ids and links them When writing tests for that parsers i get this error nomad search QueryValidationError results eln lab ids is not a doc quantity why is it not a doc quantity i also get annother validation error nomad search QueryValidationError upload id is not a doc quantity So during your test you are doing an API call or using the nomad search module I guess I don t think that by default any of the search infrastructure is setup for tests as typically you only work with the data contained in the archive itself That is probably why you are getting that error But I could probably say more if I see the test code that you are trying to run I think the safest and fastest choice for tests is to mock some of the API search calls You can learn more about mocking with pytest here https docs pytest org en stable how to monkeypatch html The other alternative is that in your test pipelines you really do setup the whole required infrastructure but that might get tricky my parsers use the nomad search module the test it self is basically just matching a file to the parser i will try with the mocks thanks that worked thanks

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: FAIRmat NFDI nomad measurements blob main nomad yaml https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad measurements blob main src nomad measurements nomad plugin yaml https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad measurements blob main src nomad measurements xrd nomad plugin yaml https github com FAIRmat NFDI nomad measurements blob main src nomad measurements xrd parser nomad plugin yaml I do not know how those are loaded You simply try to import the package before the download call If the package cannot be imported then there is no way to find the schemas you need to make sure those definitions can be located Keep in mind there are two environents at play One is on the server the other is on the client side At the moment there is no schema transfer to the client So also the client needs to have the schema available to instantiate it with the retrieved data The default jupyter lab north tool does not have any plugins installed For now you will need to manually pip install the necessary plugins at the moment There are several ways how we could mitigate this in the future put official FAIRmat plugins into the jupyter lab build put out a distribution python package with nomad lab the offficial FAIRmat plugins allows to transfer schemas with the archive query this could also be a fall back to other solutions but you will loose any normalize functions in the transfer If you cannot wait you can put a pip install cell into your notebooks have your own jupyter lab image with plugins for the IKZ oasis Ah I see it makes a lot of sense I installed the plugin in the Jupyter env and ArchiveQuery works like a charm However installing it this way isn t convenient because if the plugin is not pip installable one needs to pull the plugin repo inside Jupyter and install it locally allows to transfer schemas with the archive query this could also be a fall back to other solutions but you will loose any normalize functions in the transfer This is a nice solution as it reduces the requirements on the client side At least the user can get the structured data object allowing it to be an alternative to requests parse installing the plugin only becomes necessary when normalize functions are to be used

title: displaying non-symmetric tensors in GUI
text: Hi all does the following code mean that our matrix renderer always swaps rows cols gui src components archive visualizations js let value shape length 1 rowIndex columnIndex values columnIndex rowIndex columnIndex values columnIndex if invert value shape length 1 rowIndex columnIndex values rowIndex columnIndex rowIndex values rowIndex invert is only applied to 1 D vectors so every 2 D tensor e g 3 3 NMR stress dielectric is displayed transposed compared to the stored archive data To sum up why does the table widget transpose the tensor Please also find attached a screenshot from a user Lauri Himanen or mohammad nakhaee could help here In the case of a 1D array there is no change in how the actual value is accessed However for 2D arrays setting invert effectively transposes the matrix by swapping rowIndex and columnIndex when accessing values invert only affects how the data is visualized not the actual stored data The decision to apply invert is determined by the schema different theories and methods may represent tensors differently mohammad nakhaee thank you very much What would be then the best practice for the use cases in which we wish to skip transposing the tensors in the GUI because we need exactly the tensor in the archive and showing a Matrix T would be scientifically false e g if we let the parser transpose the data as Matrix T GUI displays the data correctly but the stored data in the archive is now transposed if we let the parser parse the data as Matrix the stored data is fine but GUI implies otherwise In general I m really not sure why the UI should transpose the data I guess it is a matter of how one is used to seeing the data displayed and stored row major order and column major order and the original person who implemented the visualization Markus made a choice I have zero objections in getting rid of the inversion directly in Javascript Could you Esma create a MR for this and I review Lauri Himanen Can you give Esma access to the mpcdf or instructions on how to get an account access she does not have this yet I can quickly reverse the transpose as well it would be great if if you could do it but for future reference in which we might need small modifications like this it d be

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Would it somehow be possible to decide if a widget appears in an app depending if it is populated by any data points given the current set of filters So e g to make empty widgets invisible Maybe Lauri Himanen has an idea if this could be possible A possible alternative more straight forward would be to have parallel apps with more locked filters I was planning this for the solar cell ones Then I would day that the app navigation menu would need some update Since the dashboard is designed to have a specific layout we probably should not simply remove empty widgets I think the better alternative is either parallel apps or parallel dashboards parallel dashboards like in here This could make sense this looks really good Is this already part of a MR Pepe Marquez this is actually a screenshot of the legacy NOMAD should have know the color https nomad lab eu prod rae gui search how difficult is it to adapt the app with parallel dashboards Not very hard Could you give an example of the use case that you have in mind I would just want to understand the use case better to see if a separate app or parallel dashboards makes more sense the current catalysis app on the oasis has a lot of widgets on one page It would be great if we could make it less overwhelming for the user by allowing them to choose the relevant widgets by e g grouping them The widgets at the moment have several themes from top to bottom reaction name reactant product catalyst properties elemental comp preparation method catalyst type reaction conditions temp gas concentrations pressure whsv reaction results scatterplots with T vs Conversion T vs Selectivity 2 plots with Conversion of a specific example reactant vs Selectivity of a specific product Especially one of the last 2 plots will be empty if some filter has been set at the top I think with either parallel dashboards we can keep the whole page less overwhelming or maybe with some toggle hide buttons for a group of widgets so that they only appear if the users chooses so see them But the filters will still be relevant from one group of widgets to the next so I dont think parallel apps is what we need in this case Let me know if I can explain

title: improve usability of term widgets with many entries
text: I would like a way to explore the terms in a term widget that are not showing as the top x items Currently the only way is to extend the size of the widget which quickly becomes impractial Is it possible to add a scrollbar to explore the less frequent appearing terms Another idea I have is to state the number of total items currently it only says e g Showing top 3 items and there doesn t seem to be a way to know the length of the list except once the widget is large enough to show all items So it would be nice to see e g Showing top 3 of 300 items or whatever the total number is Lauri Himanen How difficult would it be Should I open an issue for this Thanks Hi Julia I think it does make sense But I would only load more items if the user explicitly requests them with a button or maybe there should be a new terms widget parameter that states how many to load by default I could also see if I can get the total number of items Please create a new issue out of this and assign me Great thanks I opened the issue I have found out in the meantime that there is already the option to have autocomplete function enabled in the app widget if the term has an added elastisearch annotation suggestion default So it seems to me with this option already more terms are loaded in the gui That is a different feature that loads some suggestions that match your input It is very useful if you have hundreds thousands of options which you would not want to scroll through Do you still think that scrolling would be important now that you are aware of the suggestion option it would be helful but has not a high priority anymore I would think

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Hi all I have a student working on a mini thesis where we want to try doing some machine learning from general NOMAD data and in practice most of the work is figuring how we should filter the results to get only data with reasonable methodology and precision so that we are not training on complete crap The results precision parameters like k line density planewave cutoff etc are quite helpful for us Obviously the parser support is not always great but I think we could help there a bit at least for the cases we need However what I noticed is that specifically the k line density parameter is sometimes wrong I think there are at least two issues The parameter itself is the minimum density calculated from x y z k points k mesh grid and reciprocal cell vectors as expected The issues seems to be that when parser doesn t provide the k mesh grid but only the list of k points In that case the specific logic to construct the k mesh grid from k points doesn t IMO work in two specific cases https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob develop nomad normalizing method py ref type heads L367 and lines below have the exact logic 1 symmetry is completely ignored parsers output only the irreducible list of k point into k mesh points For example OpenMX doesn t set method k mesh but in eigenvalues sets the k mesh points the values are actually printed only for irreducible k points We don t set either multiplicities nor weights because the code doesn t print them but I can add them OpenMX only uses the inversion symmetry so multiplicity is 1 for Gamma and 2 for anything else at least for non collinear non spinorbit Therefore in practice https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob develop nomad normalizing method py ref type heads L401 k mesh grid len set k mesh points i for i in range 3 will for example end with 8 8 4 grid while the original grid was 8 8 8 and it was reduced with inversion symmetry Thus we have half the k line density we should have 2 Obviously the k mesh will be completely wrong for non uniform grids but hopefully this should be fringe enough to not be a big concern

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: original grid was 8 8 8 and it was reduced with inversion symmetry Thus we have half the k line density we should have 2 Obviously the k mesh will be completely wrong for non uniform grids but hopefully this should be fringe enough to not be a big concern but another check that the k points are uniformly spaced could be not that difficult 3 if the k mesh grid is provided but not the points the points will be reconstructed at least for Gamma centered mesh https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob develop nomad normalizing method py ref type heads L378 points np meshgrid np linspace 0 1 n for n in k mesh grid This however will create the full grid so again will be inconsistent with the parsers which provide the symmetry reduced ones into points so IMO we should save this one into all points and duplicate them into points only in case of P1 symmetry 4 https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR blob develop nomad normalizing method py ref type heads L370 k mesh dimensionality will be 3 for all of 1x1x1 1x1x6 and 20x20x1 grids is this expected and it is the way the metainfo definition is intended What I think we should do we can take a stab at some of this but at this point I m looking for a agreement on prefered way to move forward here Only construct the k mesh grid if either all points or points and one of multiplicities or weights are provided or points are provided and all mupltiplicities are one In the second case we could use the normalized reciprocal cell get list of symmetry operations and extend the points to all points while double checking that the weights multiplicities match And only from all points we could try guessing the grid preferably after also checking the uniform spacings If multiplicities weights are not provided we just abort IMO its better to provide no k line density value than a wrong value Also some questions about my uderstanding of the metainfo in the end What is actually the difference between k mesh weights and k mesh multiplicities And just to be sure about the metainfo definitions n points len all points sum multiplicities right CC Nathan Daelman Nathan Daelman sorry for double CC wasn t sure which

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: uderstanding of the metainfo in the end What is actually the difference between k mesh weights and k mesh multiplicities And just to be sure about the metainfo definitions n points len all points sum multiplicities right CC Nathan Daelman Nathan Daelman sorry for double CC wasn t sure which one is the correct one as the author of the original code in the normalizer and looking for feedback Hi Pavel thank you for picking this particular issue apart so well The k line density was originally envisioned to be quite restraint but I agree that either a its limits should be extended or b better demarcated The former is obviously nicer but hinges heavily on adding k space symmetry analysis as you indicated I have to see with Joseph Rudzinski when I take this Or maybe it gets delegated to somebody else Regardless thank you for tagging me We ll get back to you early next week is that fine Yeah this would be much appreciated but as I said for the things I need I can send patches I just wanted to check beforehand about the big picture In fact ChemaP already pointed out that https github com nomad coe nomad simulations could be the the way forward and I ll be meeting him Monday 10AM to discuss so what I m looking for is more of a feedback or general consensus how to fix this in a way that is acceptable upstream Yes we talk on Monday Nathan can help on the fix but I just want to introduce Pavel the new schema I also invited Nathan Daelman to our meeting but again it is not super necessary to be there at this first stage Sorry it was easy for me to overlook things today as I am on day off and I was outside Pavel Ondra ka it seems that this issue has landed with me Most important for me is to know your time frame as I ll be going on holidays for 2 weeks next Monday Regarding the specifics are you most interested in OpenMX data or also that from other code I m asking since the fix may involve both the normalizer and the parser Going over each of your points 1 There are actually 2 issues here 1 One is where we read the k points from the schema Our new schema

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: OpenMX data or also that from other code I m asking since the fix may involve both the normalizer and the parser Going over each of your points 1 There are actually 2 issues here 1 One is where we read the k points from the schema Our new schema will always have a single storage place and reference it where necessary In the old schema it might be more interesting to give the parser control over which place to read from 2 The other is symmetry I think that unfolding the grid goes beyond the scope of a fix though we may consider it as a feature in the future I would see to leverage the multiplicities here You mentioned that for OpenMX these would trivial to set all 1 I assume 2 Fully agreed I ll see to add this check 3 If we fix point 1 wouldn t that also resolve this issue 4 Very astute observation I d say it depends if a 2D system is modeled in 3D the extra dimension should not count If it is truly 3D then the dimension with only 1 k point may indeed be the precision bottleneck and k line density should point that out Typically codes are pretty clear on a system s dimensionality but some e g VASP always defaults to 3D or the user does not leverage the dimension control well Luckily we have a package that ascertains the dimensionality independently MatID by Lauri Himanen Now it just becomes a question of the normalization order MethodNormalizer has to come after We might also call the analyzer 2x but it s a pretty expensive routine Pavel Ondra ka I ve been working on supporting your feedback especially the symmetry reduced grid I don t think that we can cover all cases with multiplicities unless we unfold the symmetrized grid Definitely simple symmetries like cubic and orthorhombic are feasible within a short time frame Do you know or could you point me to which brillouin zones are symmetry reduced in OpenMX In general many codes only support the simpler cases I also have a hunch of how to extend the k line density to something like a k density I have to flesh it out a bit more but given that your student is using it I d be interested in your opinion there some time

title: no references resolved by the API for references to nexus package
text: nomad lab nomad FAIR blob develop nomad datamodel datamodel py ref type heads blame 0 L1037 returns a dictionary with the references in it and neither an MProxy nor a list as expected below the dict then contains the proper reference and not an MPRoxy so the final fix if isinstance current value dict current value ref value for ref in current value values Although it may should sit inside MSection m get The first MetainfoReferenceError is caused by the wrong module name which is used to resolve the definition The second issue is about collecting references so the function does not account for references using full storage If this is about the History card then it should only be picking Activities by design Thanks No it was about the Entry references card but the bug actually was that entry references into metadata section were not picked up anymore from Quantites using variadic names It is now fixed

title: nomad.yaml: issue with new plugin mechanism
text: Sarthak Kapoor and I encountered some issue when trying to run a plugin which was created with the new plugin mechanism from the template showed in last weeks tutorial locally on a nomad development environment Hampus Näsström helped us figure out what s the problem More as documentation for others multiple thing were found Plugins with the new mechanism don t need to be listed under the plugins options section in the nomad yaml It seems being pip install ed is enough On the contrary to list the plugin there leads to an error ValueError The python package nomad UBIK plugin cannot be loaded AttributeError NoneType object has no attribute get filename Furthermore the plugins include section of the nomad yaml apparently is an include only I wasn t aware of that tbh The only way to get our plugin running was to omit it completely which includes every available plugin Then it is correctly shown in the metainfo browser or the about information page We have not found a way yet to use it with the include section Maybe somebody knows more Hi there Joseph Rudzinski was working on the docu Did you check it I am curious if this is a problem of installing nomad locally that the steps are not so clear wrt to include them in an Oasis Exactly This should only affect the plugins in a local development environment of nomad as for the docker oasis image the options are clearly listed https nomad lab eu prod v1 staging docs howto oasis plugins install html There is a docu page for including plugins in your local installation Let s see if Joe can say smthing about it I can check now at home Ok it seems things moved around after Lauri s changes Joe has a branch where he took notes on how to properly clone a plugin and set it up with the local installation https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR merge requests 1813 Sorry for the delay ChemaP has it exactly correct Laurie had priority to make changes so I had to wait to integrate my changes in the above branch and I haven t yet had the chance I hope to integrate these soon and will also consider your post here Fabian Schöppach thank you

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text: Hi NOMAD team To have a non editable Quantity in NOMAD there are different ways One way is to leave out the a eln part at Quantity definition like python lab id Quantity type str description An ID string that is unique at least for the lab that produced this data But it appears a bit ugly in the GUI and might be the choise if there is a need for some indexing needed by code or a query and not for to be looked up by the user Another way from the documentation should be python name Quantity type str description A short human readable and descriptive name a eln dict component StringEditQuantity label name a display dict editable False But that seems not to work If I try it I am still able to edit Finally I can also do it in the section s m def python class MySchemaName Entity EntryData m def Section a eln dict properties dict editable dict exclude name lab id This works and also can be done with inherited attributes Can someone tell me why approach 2 is not working and what is the recommended way to do it From the docs that should indeed work mohammad nakhaee any idea why it doesn t Hi Matthias Zelt You should be able to use something like python name Quantity type str description A short human readable and descriptive name a eln dict component StringEditQuantity label name a display dict editable exclude name or more properly python name Quantity type str description A short human readable and descriptive name a eln dict component StringEditQuantity label name a display DisplayAnnotation Filter exclude name sorry I meant the a display should be added to the section definition where the quantity lives Ok What you stated above does not work Adding it to the section is basically what I wrote above 3rd option isn t it However documentation still says it can also be defined with the Quantity DisplayAnnotation is intended for the Section level not for Quantity It is as a filter of quantity names Ok thanks But then you should change the doc OK I was not aware of this so it should be a bug if it doesnt work

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: Hello I have an entry with a quantity that can also contain np nan dtype float64 When accessing this entry via API with requests everything works as expected But when using the ArchiveQuery from nomad client archive import ArchiveQuery the np nan becomes a problem Because the error TypeError Cannot cast array data from dtype O to dtype float64 according to the rule safe does not let me download the entry I was told that a related np nan problem has been discussed here https gitlab mpcdf mpg de nomad lab nomad FAIR issues 2128 Can there also be a solution for the ArchiveQuery Theodore Chang DM BITTE There is no way to tell whether a None represents nan or inf when processing a given dict And the typing system does not allow this beahviour due to data safety What you can do turn on as plain dict True so that download as plain dicts Work with plain dicts if possible If you need EntryArchives you need to manually replace affected nones with either nan or inf Then call m from dict reference code can be seen as attached Ok thank you for the input I want to resolve references from my entry so the EntryArchive would be nice I will give it a try with m from dict I forget to mention that if you have write access to the source code you can modify this block of code in ArchiveQuery There is a hidden flag treat none as nan so you can simply change line 476 into py result EntryArchive m from dict response json index archive m context context treat none as nan True Thank you very much treat none as nan works great for now
